title: RFC-014 company fact fast path
description: Implement RFC-014: simple company-fact fast path and KB ranking for short company/company contacts queries. Scope includes agent deterministic fast path, stricter payload relevance validation, canonical company-fact query rewrite on primary path, targeted DB alias/ranking improvements, tests, and benchmark coverage.

title: RFC-025 review: fail closed when required merged catalog is absent
description: Review P1. With ROUTING_CATALOG_REQUIRED=true, load_routing_index must not fall back to repo manifests or bootstrap when the active merged runtime catalog is missing or invalid. select_route and build_route_selector_payload call load_routing_index directly, so production must fail closed consistently with /health/routing. Acceptance: when ROUTING_CATALOG_REQUIRED=true and catalog.v1.json is absent, load_routing_index raises RouteCatalogUnavailable; select_route returns temporary_unavailable; build_route_selector_payload also propagates unavailability; repo/published fallback remains available only when ROUTING_CATALOG_REQUIRED is false; regression tests cover required=true with repo manifests present.

title: Implement ranking and response assembly for broad-object recommendations
description:
Source: RFC-008

Build the core recommendation logic after sphere resolution. This task owns candidate ranking, payload assembly, and the answer-oriented material returned to the agent.

Scope:

- add per-application boosts and penalties for sports high-power, quarry/heavy-duty, airport/apron, warehouse, office, and aggressive-environment scenarios
- assemble compact recommended_lamps entries with url, image_url, preview, agent_summary, facts, and recommendation_reason
- include 1-2 portfolio examples tied to the resolved application sphere
- generate one bounded follow_up_question appropriate for the scenario

Acceptance:
- high-power scenarios do not return indoor low-power linear products when suitable outdoor/projector candidates exist
- the payload contains 2-3 useful recommendations, links, images, portfolio examples, and one clarifying question
- the output is compact enough for direct agent use without extra retrieval steps
close_reason: Implemented ranking, compact recommendation payloads, portfolio examples, and follow-up questions

Fix any additional leaking tools found during the audit and document the allowed contract for retrieval tools.

title: Add unit and integration coverage for application-driven recommendation flow

title: Use LAD label during pending assistant state

title: Make agent-web layout fit iframe bounds
description: The embedded widget currently uses viewport-based sizing rules such as 100vh and calc(100vh-...) that are appropriate for a standalone page but cause the rendered chat surface to exceed the iframe height and introduce extra scrolling. Rework the widget shell so it sizes itself relative to the iframe/container instead of the browser viewport: the page root, assistant shell, and chat layout should fill the available parent height, while scrollable overflow is constrained to the message list. Scope includes removing or replacing viewport-coupled min-height and height rules, preserving the existing composer and message layout, and ensuring the resulting widget behaves correctly inside the configured iframe height without page-level overflow.

title: RFC-027 follow-up: complete documents family schema and subtype routing
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-026: add sphere context lifecycle and scoped curated-category enums
description:
Goal
- Support immediate follow-up questions inside a resolved sphere without leaking stale sphere context into unrelated requests.

Work
- Define runtime `resolved_sphere_context` state with at least:
  - `sphere_id`
  - `sphere_name`
  - `source_route_id`
  - `source_message_id` or turn identifier
  - `confidence`
  - `confirmed`
- Persist the context only after successful high-confidence sphere resolution or explicit user clarification.
- Inject scoped curated-category enum only when the current request is a local follow-up and the sphere context is still valid.
- Clear sphere context when:
  - the user names another sphere
  - the user switches to unrelated global queries (model/SKU/company/doc lookup etc.)
  - the context ages out after the agreed number of turns
  - a low-confidence context was never confirmed
- Wire this behavior into route selector payload construction and relevant follow-up route flows.

Acceptance notes
- Follow-up questions like `покажи модели из этой категории` can use scoped enums after a sphere answer.
- Unrelated later turns do not reuse stale sphere context.

Dependencies
- Depends on the core route-contract task because scoped category enums build on those contracts.
- Depends on the curated sphere categories tools-api task because the context is primarily produced by sphere-resolving routes.
close_reason: Implemented

title: legacy cleanup: migrate prompts tests and bench to canonical doc-search naming
description:
Goal
Remove legacy corp-wiki naming from agent-facing prompts, tests, and benchmark fixtures so the canonical naming is consistently `doc-search` / `doc_search`.

Why
The repo now treats `doc-search` as the canonical document retrieval path, but legacy names still appear in prompts, tests, and eval fixtures. This keeps the model exposed to two competing names for the same capability and makes later alias removal riskier.

Scope
- Update system prompt and skill-facing instructions to use only `doc-search` / `doc_search` except in explicit deprecation notes.
- Update unit/integration tests that assert or mention `corp-wiki-md-search` / `corp_wiki_search` when they are not specifically compatibility tests.
- Update bench fixtures, golden data, and routing assertions to prefer canonical naming.
- Keep a very small compatibility test surface only where backward-compat behavior is intentionally preserved.

Out of scope
- Removing the runtime alias implementation itself.
- Migrating legacy corpus files or storage paths.

Acceptance criteria
- Default prompts and routing docs no longer instruct or suggest the legacy alias as a normal path.
- Tests and bench assets use canonical names unless a test is explicitly labeled as backward-compat coverage.
- Repo-wide search shows legacy names only in clearly marked compatibility coverage, migration docs, or historical RFCs.
close_reason: Prompts, skills, tests, and bench fixtures now use canonical doc-search/doc_search naming; legacy alias references removed from active agent-facing surfaces.

title: Implement RFC-023 voice ASR stability hardening
description:
RFC-023 tracks stabilization of production voice ASR after confirming intermittent upstream Cloudflare challenge responses on chatgpt.com/backend-api/transcribe.

Scope:
- reduce user-facing failures in Telegram voice transcription;
- improve error semantics and operational observability;
- evolve from minimal mitigation to hardened current path and finally to stable API-first backend.

Planned phases:
1. Variant 1: minimal mitigation on current route.
2. Variant 2: harden current chatgpt_compat path with observability and credential protection.
3. Variant 3: move production to stable API-first ASR backend.
close_reason: Canceled at user request; ASR is currently working well

title: routing index: replace heuristic doc routing with route cards
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends replacing heuristic routing with a first-class routing index built from `corp_db` topics and document corpus topics.

Problem
Routing still depends on prompt wording, keyword heuristics, and guardrails more than on a clear retrieval catalog.

Scope
- Define route-card schemas for `corp_db_search` and `doc_search`.
- Publish document route cards from document manifests / sidecars.
- Add support for aggregated routing index loading in `core`.
- Make agent routing consult the routing index before choosing `corp_db_search` vs `doc_search`.
- Preserve explicit document/quote/fragment overrides.

Implementation notes
- The routing index should be explainable and inspectable on disk.
- Guardrails remain valuable but should sit on top of index-driven choices, not replace them.
- Start with lexical/tag-based matching; embeddings are out of scope.

Acceptance criteria
- `core` can load a routing index containing both DB and document topics.
- Representative company-fact queries route to `corp_db_search` via index lookup.
- Representative document-backed queries route to `doc_search` via index lookup.
- Routing no longer relies solely on hard-coded heuristics for these cases.
close_reason: Added route-card index generation, loading, and agent guardrails for corp_db/doc_search selection.

title: doc_search: decouple read path from writable /data/corp_docs state
description:
Context
`doc_search` currently assumes it can create directories and append usage stats under `/data/corp_docs` even for ordinary read-only search requests. In practice this makes retrieval depend on write permissions and on pre-created storage roots. The issue is visible in local verification where scripts using default `/data/corp_docs` fail with `PermissionError` on hosts without writable `/data`.

Problem
- `search_documents()` reaches `_discover_documents()`, which calls `ensure_document_layout()` and performs `mkdir` on every request.
- `tool_doc_search()` always calls `append_usage_stat()`, which also creates directories and writes JSONL even if the tool is only reading existing docs.
- This breaks read-only deployments, cold instances where storage is not initialized yet, and any environment where `/data/corp_docs` exists but is intentionally mounted read-only for the agent path.

Scope
- Split read-path discovery from write-path initialization.
- Make `doc_search` usable when live/cas/legacy roots already exist but write access is unavailable.
- Degrade usage-stats persistence gracefully when storage is read-only instead of failing the whole request.
- Introduce explicit initialization path for intake/maintenance scripts that need to create directories.

Implementation notes
- Retrieval should not call `ensure_document_layout()` unless strictly necessary for reading an optional path that already exists.
- Usage logging should be best-effort and non-fatal.
- Keep intake scripts free to initialize layout as before.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` succeeds in a read-only environment with pre-existing corpus roots.
- Missing write permission for usage-stats path does not fail retrieval.
- Intake/maintenance scripts still create layout when needed.
- Add regression tests covering read-only retrieval and non-fatal stats write failure.
close_reason: Fixed

title: document intake security: tighten policy for legacy Office binary formats
description:
Context
The intake layer currently allows legacy binary Office formats (`.doc`, `.xls`, `.ppt`) but only applies macro/archive validation to ZIP-based formats (`.docx`, `.xlsx`, `.pptx`). This leaves an under-specified and weaker security posture exactly on the highest-risk file family.

Problem
- `.doc/.xls/.ppt` are in the allowlist with no equivalent validation for macros, encryption, or malformed binary content.
- The current code explicitly comments that these formats remain allowed and are expected to rely on later parsing paths.
- This conflicts with the RFC security model for hostile document intake and can create a risky ingest path for binary Office files.

Scope
- Revisit the allowlist policy for legacy Office binary formats.
- Choose and implement one explicit v1 policy: reject by default, or accept only behind an additional validation/conversion boundary.
- Document machine-readable rejection reasons for unsupported/risky binary Office files.
- Add tests for the chosen policy.

Implementation notes
- Simplicity and reliability matter more than broad format coverage in v1.
- If the safe path is not yet implemented, rejecting legacy binary Office files is preferable to silently accepting them.

Acceptance criteria
- Legacy Office binary handling is explicit, not accidental.
- Intake no longer accepts risky formats without equivalent controls.
- Rejection/acceptance behavior is documented in code and verified by tests.
close_reason: Fixed

title: RFC-026: update core route contracts, route cards, and compact enums
description:
Goal
- Update core routing contracts so the selector can choose the new route kinds and use compact enums for stable domains.

Work
- Extend route-card and tool-arg validation to allow the new `corp_db_search.kind=sphere_curated_categories`.
- Add compact `sphere` enum from `db/spheres.json`.
- Add compact `mounting_type` enum from `db/mounting_types.json` on routes where it is semantically correct.
- Add compact `series` enum from the canonical 7-series source on series-aware routes.
- Add or update route cards for:
  - `corp_db.sphere_curated_categories`
  - narrowed `corp_db.portfolio_lookup`
  - preferred `corp_db.portfolio_by_sphere`
  - any category/mounting/series-aware follow-up routes that should expose enums
- Ensure category enum remains scoped and is never emitted as one global 163-value list.

Acceptance notes
- Selector payloads validate successfully with the new kind and new compact enums.
- Static payload generation never emits a global category enum.

Dependencies
- Depends on the series-catalog task for canonical `series` values.
- Depends on the schema/data migration because route contracts reference the new curated route and scoped category semantics.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-007: security hardening for document intake and parsing
description:
Context
RFC-007 explicitly treats document processing as potentially hostile input handling. Even local-first LiteParse requires deployer-side limits, validation and isolation.

Scope
- Add bounded execution boundary for parsing/search orchestration.
- Enforce file size, page count, wall-clock, CPU, memory and concurrency limits.
- Restrict converters to an allowlist (`lit`, `rga`, `ugrep`, `rg`, optional LibreOffice/ImageMagick wrappers).
- Ensure upload path never writes directly to `live`; only ingestion can promote validated docs.
- Protect CAS root from agent write access.
- Define policy for encrypted docs, macros, malformed files and rejection records.

Implementation notes
- Do not introduce a parser microservice unless needed; v1 should stay simple.
- Security controls should apply both to intake and to parse-on-demand execution.
- Avoid raw user-controlled shell fragments in any filter command.

Acceptance criteria
- Document intake and parsing run with enforceable limits.
- Agent cannot write to document live/CAS roots.
- Invalid or risky files can be rejected with machine-readable reason.
- Security model is documented and testable.
close_reason: Added bounded parser execution limits, direct /data/corp_docs deny path, safe search_text execution, rejection records, and security regression tests.

title: Replace user message label with Russian text

Scope is limited to the message bubble header label; user message alignment, colors, keyboard handling, and transport behavior should remain unchanged.

title: RFC remediation: close doc-domain retrieval and block post-answer doc browsing
description:
Problem
- RFC-016/RFC-019 require retrieval closure after sufficient evidence and a bounded tool loop.
- Current runtime closes retrieval only for authoritative `corp_db_search` KB routes.
- After successful `doc_search`, the agent still allows follow-up doc-browse tools such as `read_file` / `search_text` / `run_command`, so the doc-domain loop remains open.

Scope
- Update doc-domain runtime state in `core/agent.py` so successful `doc_search` can mark retrieval evidence as sufficient and close retrieval when the question is satisfied.
- Extend the closed-retrieval/doc-domain guardrail so post-closure document browsing tools are blocked the same way secondary retrieval tools are blocked for closed KB routes.
- Preserve the existing explicit-document-request exception only where it is intentional and RFC-compatible.
- Add regression coverage for `doc_search -> read_file` and similar follow-up attempts after a successful doc-domain lookup.

Acceptance Criteria
- After successful `doc_search` for a document-domain question, `retrieval_phase=closed` and `retrieval_evidence_status=sufficient` are visible in meta.
- A follow-up `read_file`, `search_text`, `search_files`, or `run_command` against the doc corpus is blocked and increments the routing guardrail counter.
- Existing `core.tests.test_routing_guardrail` coverage passes with new regression tests.
- No unrelated routing or observability changes are mixed into this issue.

Files Likely Touched
- `core/agent.py`
- `core/tests/test_routing_guardrail.py`
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-027: narrow route-local schemas and add stage-2 leaf routes

Implement the new route-local leaf structure from RFC-027.

Scope:

- map existing corp_db/corp_kb routes into business families without breaking executors
- adjust deterministic route ordering for broad series, document-by-lamp, and category-showcase questions

Acceptance criteria:
- company/common schema is no longer a generic mega-schema
- broad series questions prefer the dedicated series route
- catalog includes a showcase_lamps_by_category route contract
- catalog includes a documents_by_lamp_name route contract
- tests cover new routes and route-local schema narrowing

title: Remove non-user-facing widget chrome from agent-web

These labels are useful for development but waste vertical space in a production iframe and distract from the conversation itself. Remove the top hero/header block and the duplicated footer/helper copy while preserving the chat surface, visual stability, and existing message flow. Validation should confirm that the widget opens directly into the conversation area with no developer-facing text taking the first screen.

title: Fix deterministic finalization for authoritative KB route lookups
description:
Discovered during RCA verification after totosha-y1j/totosha-iir fixes:
- Query "Расскажи о серии LAD LED LINE" now gets a successful corp_db authoritative KB payload from corp_kb.company_common with topic_facets=["series"].
- The agent still hands finalization to the LLM and may answer with a false negative like "нет подтверждённой информации", contradicting the retrieved KB chunk.

Scope:
- When authoritative KB route lookup returns sufficient evidence, render a deterministic answer directly from payload instead of delegating the final interpretation to the LLM.
- Cover company_common series route and preserve explicit wiki/document requests.
- Add regression test for the LAD LED LINE/series scenario.

Acceptance:
- Live query "Расскажи о серии LAD LED LINE" no longer claims the KB has no information when the authoritative payload contains the series chunk.
- Meta shows deterministic primary finalization for authoritative KB routes where payload is sufficient.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Restore full runtime payload for corp_db company facts
description: Change core/tools/corp_db.py so company-fact kb_search returns full-fidelity JSON in ToolResult.output for production runtime. Keep compact company-fact payload only inside metadata['bench_artifact']. Remove runtime dependence on result_format=compact_company_fact_v1. Update contract comments and any helper names that imply compact runtime output. This task closes the active production leak where user-facing answers are rendered from truncated previews.
close_reason: Restored full runtime JSON output for corp_db company facts and moved compact payload to metadata only

title: Routing cleanup: route-card LLM selector without score gates
description: Epic: simplify production routing after real-query failures for realized projects and portfolio objects. The LLM selector must receive the relevant route-card catalog directly instead of a hand-scored shortlist, portfolio/project questions must route to portfolio-capable corp_db searches, and empty/weak evidence from a wrong scoped KB route must not trap the agent in repeated searches. This epic also removes obsolete score-based production routing remnants. Acceptance: questions about RZhD projects and the Белый Раст logistics center can reach portfolio data; route selector payload is not gated by score/authority; LLM outage remains fail-closed with a temporary-unavailable message; regression tests cover the flow.
close_reason: Implemented RFC-025 routing cleanup task set

title: Add portfolio entity lookup route card
description: Problem: existing portfolio_by_sphere works for broad sphere lists but is awkward for named objects such as ТЛЦ Белый Раст. Scope: add a corp_db.portfolio_lookup route card using corp_db_search kind=hybrid_search, profile=entity_resolver, entity_types=[portfolio,sphere], with clear summary/keywords/patterns for realized projects, objects, references, RZhD, logistics centers, and named portfolio objects. Keep portfolio_by_sphere for list-by-sphere queries and add fallback links between related portfolio routes. Acceptance: route catalog validates; compact selector card exposes the route and its locked args; direct route execution can find Белый Раст and RZhD portfolio entities.
close_reason: Implemented portfolio_lookup route card and related portfolio route hints

title: Remove score-gated route selector payload
description: Problem: build_route_selector_payload still uses _selector_match_weight and drops routes with weight=0. This prevented corp_db.portfolio_by_sphere/application routes from reaching the LLM for a Белый Раст query. Scope: pass all selector-visible route cards when the catalog is small enough for the compact selector budget (current target <=60 routes), preserve deterministic catalog order, and remove obsolete match-weight/prefilter code and score fields from selector payloads/prompts. If a catalog grows beyond the budget, use a transparent truncation strategy that never filters solely by authority score. Acceptance: build_route_selector_payload for Белый Раст includes portfolio and application routes; no _selector_match_weight or score prefilter remains in production selector payload construction; tests assert portfolio route visibility.
close_reason: Removed score-gated selector payload and production route scoring functions

title: Controlled portfolio fallback after weak company KB evidence
description: Problem: when the selector chooses corp_kb.company_common for a project/object query, empty or weak evidence keeps the agent bound to the same knowledge_route_id/source_files and causes repeated useless corp_db_search calls. Scope: after primary route execution returns empty/weak for company_common and the query has portfolio/project/object signals, execute one controlled corp_db portfolio lookup fallback instead of entering a repeated same-route loop. Preserve fail-closed behavior for selector/LLM outage. Acceptance: Белый Раст and RZhD project questions no longer stay trapped in common_information_about_company.md; duplicate retrieval guardrail is not the only protection; no generic document fallback is introduced.
close_reason: Implemented controlled portfolio fallback after weak/empty company_common evidence for project queries

title: Portfolio routing replay tests
description: Add regression coverage for the real failing questions and for the simplified selector contract. Cases: 'Расскажи подробнее про терминально-логистический центр Белый Раст' includes/selects portfolio-capable routes; 'Какие объекты были реализованы для РЖД?' and 'список объектов/проектов для РЖД' can use portfolio_by_sphere/entity lookup; selector payload is not reduced to company_common by score; LLM-unavailable path still returns temporary unavailable instead of score fallback. title: RFC-006: tighten corp-db default routing for company facts

- Update shared skill descriptions so corp-pg-db clearly owns company facts and corp-wiki-md-search is narrowed to non-indexed docs / explicit wiki context.

Acceptance:

- If corp_db_search(kind=hybrid_search, profile=kb_search) succeeds for a short company-fact query, block same-turn wiki-oriented tool calls and return a clear tool error instructing the model to answer from corp_db.
- Preserve wiki access when corp_db_search is empty/error or when the user explicitly requests wiki/document context.
- Emit routing observability signals that distinguish selected source, wiki-after-corp-db-success, and guardrail activations.

Acceptance:

title: RFC-026: harden portfolio evidence policy and bounded fallback
description:
Goal
- Fix the routing/evidence failures around broad portfolio questions so entity resolution does not prematurely close retrieval and empty results do not trigger noisy LLM loops.

Work
- Reclassify `hybrid_search + profile=entity_resolver` as intermediate evidence for broad portfolio asks.
- Require final portfolio-capable evidence before closing retrieval on questions like `какие объекты были реализованы для РЖД`.
- Tighten `corp_db.portfolio_lookup` semantics to named-object lookup.
- Make `corp_db.portfolio_by_sphere` the preferred broad route for portfolio-by-segment questions.
- Replace open-ended post-empty behavior with one bounded canonicalization retry plus at most one bounded fallback or clarification.
- Update tools-api sphere canonicalization order so portfolio resolution uses real sphere catalog data before partial handwritten profiles.

Acceptance notes

- Empty portfolio paths do not synthesize unrelated lamp-filter args.

Dependencies

title: RFC-007: observability and usage stats for doc_search
description:
Context
RFC-007 relies on usage statistics both for debugging retrieval quality and for deciding which documents should be promoted into `corp-db`. Without explicit metrics and traces the new search path will be hard to tune safely.

Scope
- Emit metrics for file_type, match_mode, cache_hit, duration_ms, parse_duration_ms and result_count.
- Add trace attributes / spans for backend selection and fallback path.
- Persist usage stats needed for promotion candidates: query, intent_class, document_id, selected_result_rank, answer_success when available.
- Keep backward-compatible routing observability where possible.

Implementation notes
- Stats should distinguish no-match from parser failure.
- Avoid excessive cardinality in metrics; detailed per-document data should go to logs/tables, not raw metrics labels.
- Make usage records reusable by later promotion jobs.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` requests are observable by backend mode and latency.
- Usage stats are sufficient to build promotion candidate reports.
- Failures and no-match are distinguishable in telemetry.
- New observability does not regress existing bench/debug workflows.
close_reason: Added doc_search metrics, trace attributes, backend/cache telemetry, and persisted usage stats for promotion candidates.

title: RFC-027 stage3 follow-up: implement first stage3_optimized route(s)
description:
## Context
RFC-027 defines a three-stage routing model:
- `stage1_general`
- `stage2_specialized`
- `stage3_optimized`

The current codebase implements stage-1/stage-2 routing contracts well, but there are no active `stage3_optimized` routes yet. The architecture supports stage metadata, but the route-specific optimized executor layer is still missing.

## Problem
We need a dedicated follow-up to implement the first real stage-3 optimized route(s) so RFC-027 is not only contract-complete, but also realized in the executor layer.

## Scope
1. Choose the best first candidate route(s) for stage-3 promotion, likely from:
   - `catalog/showcase_lamps_by_category`
   - `documents/documents_by_lamp_name`
   - `codes_and_sku/sku_codes_lookup`
   - `sphere_category_mapping/curated_categories_by_sphere`
   - `portfolio/portfolio_projects_by_sphere`
2. Design and implement a dedicated optimized executor/data path while keeping the public family/leaf contract stable.
3. Mark promoted route(s) with `route_stage=stage3_optimized`.
4. Add route-specific tests, replay coverage, and observability verification.
5. Update RFC/docs/runbooks so the first concrete stage-3 route is documented.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one production route is promoted to `stage3_optimized`.
- The promoted route uses a dedicated optimized executor or curated data path, not just the generic executor contract.
- Public selector/family/leaf behavior remains stable.
- Tests and observability explicitly verify `stage3_optimized` behavior.
- RFC-027 documentation reflects the concrete first stage-3 implementation.
close_reason: Implemented the first concrete RFC-027 stage3_optimized routes across showcase, documents, and codes/SKU flows.

title: Stage3 candidate: optimize catalog/showcase_lamps_by_category executor
description:
## Context
RFC-027 expects stage-3 route-specific optimization after stage-2 semantics become stable. `catalog/showcase_lamps_by_category` is a strong first candidate because it already has a dedicated leaf contract, but currently still executes through the generic `category_lamps` path.

## Problem
Representative category-example requests still rely on the generic executor/data path instead of a curated showcase-specific resolver. That limits control over ordering, boundedness, and reproducibility.

## Scope
1. Define the optimized data source for showcase-by-category answers.
2. Implement a dedicated executor/resolver for `showcase_lamps_by_category`.
3. Keep the public family/leaf contract stable.
4. Promote the route to `route_stage=stage3_optimized` when the optimized path is active.
5. Add tests and observability checks for the stage-3 path.

## Acceptance criteria
- `catalog/showcase_lamps_by_category` no longer relies only on the generic category-lamps executor.
- Results come from a curated/optimized showcase-specific path.
- Route metadata and observability report `stage3_optimized`.
- Tests cover stage-3 execution and bounded outputs.
close_reason: Promoted showcase category examples to stage3_optimized with dedicated showcase_category_lamps executor path and route/tests/docs updates.

title: Stage3 candidate: optimize documents_by_lamp_name executor and document index
description:
## Context
`documents/documents_by_lamp_name` is a natural stage-3 candidate from RFC-027 because document lookup is already a first-class family/leaf contract, but execution still runs through a generic lamp-exact path.

## Problem
Document-by-lamp retrieval does not yet use a dedicated optimized document selector/index layer. That keeps subtype retrieval and bounded fallback dependent on a broader generic executor.

## Scope
1. Design a dedicated lamp-to-document selector/index for `documents_by_lamp_name` and subtype leaves.
2. Implement the optimized executor/data path while preserving the selector contract.
3. Promote the route(s) to `route_stage=stage3_optimized` when the new path is active.
4. Add tests for general documents and subtype routes under the stage-3 path.
5. Verify observability for family/leaf/stage/fallback on optimized document retrieval.

## Acceptance criteria
- `documents/documents_by_lamp_name` (and compatible subtype flow where appropriate) uses a dedicated optimized document path.
- Public family/leaf behavior stays stable.
- Route metadata and observability report `stage3_optimized` where promoted.
- Tests cover optimized general and subtype document retrieval.
close_reason: Promoted documents_by_lamp_name and subtype flows to stage3_optimized with dedicated lamp_documents_index executor path and route/tests/docs updates.

title: Stage3 candidate: optimize sku_codes_lookup executor and reverse code index
description:
## Context
`codes_and_sku/sku_codes_lookup` is a strong stage-3 candidate from RFC-027 because code lookup semantics are already separated at the contract level, but execution still relies on a generic catalog/lamp-exact path.

## Problem
Lamp-name-to-code and reverse code lookup do not yet use a dedicated optimized index/resolver layer. This limits exactness, reversibility, and bounded behavior for ETM/ORACL/article/SKU questions.

## Scope
1. Design an optimized code lookup index/resolver for `sku_codes_lookup` and compatible reverse flows.
2. Implement the route-specific executor/data path while preserving the business contract.
3. Promote the optimized route(s) to `route_stage=stage3_optimized`.
4. Add tests for by-name, by-code, and reverse code lookup under the stage-3 path.
5. Verify route-stage and route-identity observability for optimized code lookups.

## Acceptance criteria
- `codes_and_sku/sku_codes_lookup` uses a dedicated optimized lookup path.
- By-name and reverse code flows remain stable at the selector/leaf contract level.
- Route metadata and observability report `stage3_optimized` where promoted.
- Tests cover optimized code lookup behavior and bounded fallbacks.
close_reason: Promoted codes/SKU routes to stage3_optimized with dedicated lamp_code_lookup executor path for by-name and reverse-code flows, plus route/tests/docs updates.

title: document normalization: parsed sidecars keyed by CAS hash
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends shifting parsing from query-time to ingest-time and storing normalized searchable sidecars keyed by CAS hash and parser version.

Problem
Current `doc_search` still parses raw files on the query path. There is no canonical normalized sidecar layout produced during ingest.

Scope
- Introduce `/data/corp_docs/parsed/<sha>/...` sidecars.
- Generate at least `text.txt`, `pages.jsonl`, and `meta.json` during ingest/rebuild.
- Key parsed artifacts by content hash plus parser version / OCR config hash.
- Add rebuild commands for stale or missing sidecars.

Implementation notes
- `lit` is the primary normalization backend for multi-format documents.
- Text-first formats may still use cheap direct normalization if the output contract remains identical.
- Sidecar publication must be atomic enough that `core` never sees half-written parsed artifacts.

Acceptance criteria
- Ingested documents get normalized searchable sidecars.
- Parser version changes cause rebuildable invalidation.
- Parsed artifacts are addressable by content hash, not original filename.
- Missing or stale sidecars can be rebuilt via `doc-worker`.
close_reason: Added parsed sidecars keyed by CAS hash and version, normalization on ingest, and rebuild-parsed support.

title: RFC-027 follow-up: complete routing observability for family, leaf, stage, validation, and fallback behavior
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-007: intake lifecycle and CAS-backed document storage
description:
Context
RFC-007 требует простой и надёжный document intake path с дедупликацией. Документы не должны попадать сразу в searchable corpus, а физические дубли не должны размножаться по папкам.

Scope
- Ввести filesystem lifecycle: `quarantine -> cas -> live/rejected`.
- Реализовать content-addressed storage в `/data/corp_docs/cas/` на основе `sha256`.
- Ввести manifests/metadata records, которые ссылаются на CAS blob вместо хранения независимых копий.
- Ввести thin live-record / alias model для searchable corpus.
- Определить retention policy для rejected/quarantine objects и sweep policy для unreferenced blobs.

Implementation notes
- v1 dedup только по exact content hash; near-duplicate detection не нужен.
- Atomic rename/move only; new uploads must never write directly into `live`.
- Layout должен быть sharded (`ab/cd/<sha>`), чтобы не раздувать каталоги.
- Решение должно оставаться на обычной FS без object store и distributed storage.

Acceptance criteria
- Повторная загрузка идентичного файла не создаёт второй physical blob.
- `live` содержит только metadata/aliases или thin records поверх CAS.
- Ingestion может однозначно восстановить document record по hash.
- CAS design documented in code/docs and ready for parser/cache integration.
close_reason: Implemented CAS-backed intake lifecycle with quarantine/live/rejected/manifests roots, dedup by sha256, thin live records, and maintenance scripts.

title: legacy office policy: re-enable .doc/.xls/.ppt only after guaranteed lit path
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends re-enabling legacy binary Office formats only after a guaranteed LiteParse normalization path exists.

Problem
Legacy `.doc/.xls/.ppt` are currently rejected for safety reasons. That is appropriate now, but the format policy must evolve once the new normalization stack is real.

Scope
- Add end-to-end tests for legacy binary Office formats through the guaranteed `lit + LibreOffice` path.
- Revisit allowlist/reject policy after that path is proven.
- Keep rejection reasons explicit for any still-unsupported cases.

Implementation notes
- This task depends on the worker and normalization stack being complete.
- Simplicity and safety win over broad format support if any backend remains unreliable.

Acceptance criteria
- Legacy Office support is either safely enabled via the new path or explicitly left disabled with documented reasons.
- Tests cover the chosen behavior.
- Public tool/skill descriptions match the actual runtime policy.
close_reason: Legacy doc/xls/ppt support is gated by doc-worker runtime with explicit tests and synchronized public descriptions.

title: RFC-027 cleanup: remove legacy deterministic finalizer helpers and modes from core/agent.py
description: core/agent.py still contains dead or legacy deterministic finalizer helpers and mode labels (_render_deterministic_tool_output, _build_deterministic_fallback_call, _deterministic_empty_response_fallback, finalizer_mode=deterministic_fallback/deterministic_error). RFC-027 requires LLM-finalized successful answers and fail-closed behavior on selector/finalizer unavailability. Remove the obsolete deterministic helper path, rename remaining bounded failure modes away from deterministic finalizer semantics, and align tests/observability with the cleaned contract.
close_reason: Removed legacy deterministic-finalizer labels/helpers from active RFC-027 runtime paths, renamed bounded closure modes to bounded_failure/bounded_error, updated guardrail coverage, and documented the cleaned contract.

title: RFC-006: compact corp-db company-fact payload
description:
Implement phase 2 data-contract changes from RFC-006.

Scope:
- Reduce corp_db_search payload size for company-fact retrievals (profile=kb_search with company entities) or add a dedicated compact kind such as company_fact.
- Preserve authoritative fields needed for short answers while avoiding large retrieval dumps that encourage extra LLM/tool iterations.
- Update callers/tests as needed.

Dependencies:
- Depends on routing hardening so behavior is stable before changing payload shape.

Acceptance:
- Company-fact corp_db responses are materially smaller and still sufficient for short factual answers.
close_reason: Compacted company-fact corp_db payload in core tool formatter and covered it with tests

title: RFC-005: Fast path для portfolio examples по модели светильника
description:
Контекст
Нужно реализовать fast path для сценария "примеры реализации/объекты/портфолио для светильника <точная модель>" по RFC-005 (docs/rfc/RFC-005-lamp-portfolio-examples-fast-path.md). Сейчас данные в БД есть, но нет одного детерминированного server-side path lamp -> category -> sphere -> portfolio, из-за чего агент уходит в дорогую многошаговую orchestration.

Цель
Добавить новый kind в corp_db_search и довести сценарий до быстрого, стабильного и наблюдаемого end-to-end поведения без hybrid_search/wiki/shell на happy path.

Состав работ
1. Реализовать backend contract и handler в tools-api.
2. Добавить observability для нового kind и его фаз.
3. Обновить tool schema/propagation в core.
4. Добавить routing rule в системный prompt агента.
5. Закрыть unit/integration/manual smoke.

Критерии приемки
- В системе есть kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp с контрактом по RFC-005.
- Агент может ответить на запросы вида "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" через один corp_db_search fast path.
- Для success path не используются hybrid_search, wiki и shell fallback.
- Путь диагностируем по logs/traces/metrics.
close_reason: Implemented the RFC-005 fast path end-to-end across tools-api, observability, tool definitions, agent routing, and regression coverage.

title: RFC-005: Реализовать kind portfolio_examples_by_lamp в tools-api
description:
Контекст
Нужно закрыть основную functional gap RFC-005: добавить один composed backend operation для цепочки lamp -> category -> sphere -> portfolio внутри существующего route /corp-db/search. Это ключевая задача, без нее весь fast path не существует.

Что сделать
- Добавить новый kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp в schema/validation CorpDbSearchRequest.
- Добавить dispatch branch в основном route tools-api/src/routes/corp_db.py.
- Реализовать handler _portfolio_examples_by_lamp(conn, req, limit, offset).
- Переиспользовать exact lamp resolve path от lamp_exact, чтобы получить canonical lamp row и не дублировать нестабильную логику match/serialize.
- Выполнить server-side chain: exact lamp resolve -> category lookup -> sphere lookup -> portfolio lookup.
- Возвращать compact response model по RFC: status, kind, query, filters, lamp, spheres, portfolio_examples.
- Корректно маппить empty cases: lamp_not_found, category_missing, spheres_not_found, portfolio_not_found.
- Не использовать hybrid_search, embeddings, shell или внешние источники.

Ограничения
- v1 без materialized view и без нового storage layer.
- Нужен компактный payload, чтобы не раздувать контекст агента.
- Поля lamp.preview/agent_summary/facts должны переиспользовать существующий serializer от lamp_exact.

Критерии приемки
- POST /corp-db/search принимает kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp.
- На success возвращается одна лампа, список spheres и плоский список portfolio_examples с привязкой к sphere.
- На empty path возвращается filters.reason без runtime error.
- На happy path весь сценарий выполняется в одном route без дополнительных retrieval обходов.
close_reason: Implemented portfolio_examples_by_lamp kind in tools-api with exact lamp resolve, sphere lookup, portfolio lookup, compact response contract, and empty-path handling.

title: RFC-005: Добавить observability для portfolio_examples_by_lamp
description:
Контекст
После появления нового RPC нужен отдельный наблюдаемый execution path. Цель этой задачи — сделать triage быстрым и однозначным по request_id: где именно цепочка остановилась и сколько заняли ее фазы.

Что сделать
- Добавить route-level metrics для kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp в существующую модель метрик tools-api.
- Добавить phase-level duration metrics для этапов lamp_exact, sphere_lookup, portfolio_lookup, response_build.
- Добавить child spans внутри api.request: corp_db.portfolio_examples.lamp_exact, corp_db.portfolio_examples.sphere_lookup, corp_db.portfolio_examples.portfolio_lookup.
- Логировать в structured logs поля request_id, kind, lamp_id, category_id, sphere_count, portfolio_count, status.
- Проверить, что success/empty/error paths различимы по логам и trace.

Почему зависит от backend
Сначала должен появиться сам handler и финальная execution shape, иначе observability будет навешана на нестабильный path.

Критерии приемки
- Новый kind различим в metrics и logs отдельно от прочих corp_db_search запросов.
- В trace видны child spans по ключевым фазам нового RPC.
- По одному request_id можно понять: лампа не нашлась, сферы не нашлись или портфолио пусто.
- Empty path не выглядит как transport/runtime error в signals.
close_reason: Added observability for portfolio_examples_by_lamp: phase spans, phase metrics, and structured logs with request_id/lamp/category/sphere/portfolio counts.

title: RFC-005: Обновить schema и transport для нового corp_db_search kind в core
description:
Контекст
После добавления нового kind в tools-api core должен знать о нем как о допустимом инструментальном пути. Это нужно, чтобы агент мог вызвать fast path через существующий corp_db_search client без ручных обходов.

Что сделать
- Обновить tool schema/enum и описание нового kind в core/tools/corp_db.py и связанных слоях, если это требуется текущей реализацией.
- Убедиться, что transport layer не требует специальных изменений и корректно пропускает новый kind в tools-api.
- Проверить сериализацию запроса и обработку ответа success/empty/error на стороне core tool wrapper.
- При наличии существующих тестов на corp_db tool обновить их под новый kind.

Почему зависит от backend
Сначала должен быть зафиксирован и реализован public contract нового kind в tools-api. После этого core можно синхронизировать с фактической схемой.

Критерии приемки
- Core умеет валидно вызывать kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp через corp_db_search.
- Новый kind описан в tool schema и доступен агенту.
- На стороне core нет рассинхрона между ожидаемым и фактическим contract response.
close_reason: Synced new corp_db_search kind through tool definitions and validated core transport preserves the specialized payload without contract drift.

title: RFC-005: Добавить agent routing rule для portfolio examples по точной модели
description:
Контекст
Даже после появления RPC агент не начнет использовать его стабильно без явного правила в system prompt. Текущий сценарий деградирует именно потому, что агент уходит в generic retrieval chain и fallback-поведение.

Что сделать
- Обновить core/src/agent/system.txt.
- Добавить rule для запросов про "примеры реализации", "объекты", "портфолио", "где применялся", "какие проекты были" при наличии точной модели светильника.
- Новое правило должно приоритизировать corp_db_search(kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp, name=<model>) раньше generic hybrid_search/retry/fallback веток.
- Зафиксировать поведение: при success нового RPC не вызывать руками sphere_categories, portfolio_by_sphere, hybrid_search, wiki или shell.
- Разрешить fallback только если RPC вернул empty и пользователь явно просит расширенный текстовый контекст.

Почему зависит от core schema
Prompt должен ссылаться на уже доступный и корректно описанный kind. Иначе агент получит инструкцию на несуществующий или невидимый инструментальный путь.

Критерии приемки
- Запрос вида "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" маршрутизируется в новый kind как первичный шаг.
- При success path агент не уходит в hybrid_search/wiki/shell.
- Поведение fallback ограничено и предсказуемо.
close_reason: Updated agent routing and corp-pg-db skill guidance so exact-model portfolio/example questions go straight to portfolio_examples_by_lamp and avoid expensive fallback paths.

title: RFC-005: Покрыть fast path тестами и smoke-проверками
description:
Контекст
После реализации backend path, observability и routing нужен финальный verification layer. Без этого останется риск, что контракт есть, но агент все равно уходит в старую цепочку или что empty/error paths сломаны.

Что сделать
- Добавить unit tests для handler на success, lamp_not_found, category_missing/spheres_not_found, portfolio_not_found и runtime error mapping.
- Добавить integration test на POST /corp-db/search с kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp и проверкой JSON contract.
- Проверить, что request_id проходит через logs/traces для нового kind.
- Выполнить manual smoke на сценариях: exact model with portfolio, exact model without portfolio, unknown model.
- Отдельно подтвердить, что запрос "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" идет через один corp_db_search fast path без hybrid_search/wiki/shell на happy path.

Почему зависит от observability и routing
Финальная проверка должна подтверждать не только backend contract, но и реальное поведение системы end-to-end, включая трассировку и agent routing.

Критерии приемки
- Unit и integration tests покрывают success/empty/error paths нового kind.
- Manual smoke подтверждает ожидаемое поведение по типовым сценариям.
- Есть явное подтверждение, что happy path не использует старые дорогие fallback-механизмы.
close_reason: Added regression tests for the new fast path, validated current workspace with tools-api/core test runs, and covered the key success/empty observability paths.

title: Compact agent-web composer for iframe layout
description: The current composer uses a stacked layout with helper text on one row and the send button on another, which increases the bottom chrome height inside the iframe. Rework the composer into a denser single-row composition where the input remains the primary element and the send button stays aligned with it on the same visual row. Keep keyboard send behavior, disabled/pending states, and mobile usability intact while reducing consumed vertical space. Validation should confirm the footer is visibly shorter and the send action aligns with the input rather than sitting below it.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RCA: agent false-negative on company facts and series info
description:
Analyze traces, logs, metrics, and runtime artifacts for failures like "Подскажи контакты компании" and "Расскажи о серии LAD LED LINE".

Context:
- Live reproduction on 2026-04-10 via POST /api/chat confirmed:
  - route_id=corp_kb.company_common
  - corp_db_search called twice with knowledge_route_id=corp_kb.company_common and query=239-18-11 lad@ladled.ru контакты ladzavod
  - tool output returned success=true but payload status=error both times
  - model then attempted doc_search; first blocked by guardrail, later allowed; doc_search matched irrelevant DOC fragment
  - final answer falsely claimed corporate DB unavailable
- Need root cause and concrete remediation tasks.

Acceptance:
- Identify precise failure mode(s) in corp_db/doc_search/agent finalization path.
- Add follow-up implementation issues in br with dependencies.
- Close this issue only after root cause is documented in child issues or comments.
close_reason: Implemented

title: legacy cleanup: final audit and removal gate for corp-wiki references
description:
Goal
Run a final repo-wide audit after migration tasks complete and remove the last non-historical corp-wiki references.

Why
Legacy names tend to survive in edge-case configs, tests, scripts, or docs. A dedicated cleanup gate reduces the chance of shipping a half-removed alias that later reappears in prompts or runtime.

Scope
- Perform repo-wide audit for `corp-wiki-md-search` and `corp_wiki_search`.
- Remove any remaining non-historical references in code, scripts, tests, docs, or bench assets.
- Confirm inventories and generated docs are consistent with canonical naming.
- Record any intentionally retained historical references and why they remain.

Dependencies
- Depends on the naming migration, legacy path migration, and alias removal tasks.

Acceptance criteria
- Repo-wide search for legacy corp-wiki names returns only intentionally retained historical references, if any.
- No active runtime, prompt, test, or operator flow depends on the legacy alias.
- Cleanup result is documented in issue closure notes or a short migration note.
close_reason: Repo-wide audit completed; remaining active code paths are clean of corp-wiki legacy names outside historical artifacts.

title: Extract routing policy helpers from core agent
description: Problem: core/agent.py still owns duplicated routing business knowledge: COMPANY_FACT_KEYWORDS, DOCUMENT_LOOKUP_KEYWORDS, company facet classification, document/portfolio/application intent helpers, and deterministic KB rewrite helpers. This keeps the architecture split after RFC-025. Scope: introduce a shared routing policy module under core/documents/ or equivalent; move remaining keyword/facet/rewrite helper logic used by agent into that module; update core/agent.py imports and tests; keep behavior unchanged. Avoid touching tool execution or answer finalization. Acceptance: core/agent.py no longer defines the duplicated keyword tuples or company common facet/rewrite helpers directly; tests for certification, quality, portfolio fallback, selector validation, and routing guardrails pass.
close_reason: Implemented

title: make core corp_db_search timeouts and transport errors diagnosable
description:
RFC-004 task 3. Harden core-side tool execution for corp_db_search so slow or failed calls do not surface as empty errors.

Deliverables:
- include exception class and timeout budget in ToolResult.error for corp_db_search failures
- split aiohttp timeout budget into connect/read/total settings
- reassess total timeout after backend fast path changes
- add focused tests for TimeoutError and transport failures
close_reason: Added diagnosable corp_db_search timeout and transport errors in core with split timeout budget and focused tests.

title: Variant 1: classify challenge errors, retry, and normalize voice UX
close_reason: Implemented minimal mitigation in bot voice path with challenge classification, bounded retry, normalized user messaging, and unit tests.

title: Make latency chart resilient to long case labels
description: Update the bench dashboard layout so the 'Latency by Case (ms)' chart no longer collapses when case_id labels are long. Move the latency chart into its own row beneath Outcome and Tags, increase the chart card height, and adjust ECharts grid/axis label behavior so Y-axis labels remain readable and X-axis labels do not steal excessive plotting space. Acceptance: latency chart has a dedicated wide layout, axis labels remain legible on current dataset, and dashboard still works on desktop and mobile widths.
close_reason: Completed

title: RFC-012 benchmark algorithmic validation
close_reason: RFC-012 implemented: artifact capture, algorithmic validation, comparison mode, direct_tool runner, dataset migration, and tests are complete.

close_reason: Implemented bounded artifact capture in core via run_meta, tool metadata, and /api/chat meta exposure.

close_reason: Extended bench dataset/result schema with execution/validation modes and artifact-aware result rows.

close_reason: Added algorithmic artifact evaluator with path resolver, selectors, and structured checks.

close_reason: Implemented direct_tool mode in bench runner for deterministic corp_db benchmark cases.

close_reason: Migrated golden benchmark dataset to execution/validation modes with 38 algorithmic cases.

close_reason: Added unit and comparison tests for artifact capture, evaluator, comparison, and direct_tool rows.

title: Add company-fact routing telemetry fields
description: Add low-cost observability fields for the company-fact fast path, including company_fact_intent_type, company_fact_fast_path, company_fact_payload_relevant, company_fact_rendered, and company_fact_fallback_reason. This task is limited to local metadata/log enrichment and does not implement the broader RFC-013 observability work.
close_reason: Added local company-fact telemetry fields in run meta and span attributes

title: RFC-026: add tests, bench coverage, and rollout checks
description:
Goal
- Lock RFC-026 behavior down with automated coverage and rollout validation before enabling it broadly.

Work
- Add unit tests for:
  - curated sphere-category seeding
  - parent category persistence
  - compact sphere/mounting_type/series enums
  - scoped curated-category enum injection and expiry
  - portfolio evidence status rules
- Add integration tests for:
  - `Какие категории подходят для склада?`
  - `Какие категории подходят для РЖД?`
  - broad portfolio questions for `РЖД` and industrial sphere wording
  - follow-up flows that rely on sphere context
- Update bench/golden cases for curated sphere categories and portfolio routing regressions.
- Add rollout checks or smoke validation steps for migrated environments and route observability.

Acceptance notes
- RFC-026 scenarios are covered in focused tests and representative bench cases.
- Migration and rollout can be validated repeatably.

Dependencies
- Depends on all implementation tasks in this epic.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Add benchmark and live agent cases for company facts
description: Extend benchmark coverage with explicit company-fact end-to-end cases such as 'Расскажи о компании' and 'Подскажи контакты компании.' Validate the live agent path rather than direct-tool only, so the benchmark catches false-negative final answers after successful corp_db payloads.
close_reason: Added live agent benchmark cases for company facts and validated focused benchmark subset

title: RFC-007: promotion pipeline from doc_search corpus to corp-db
description:
Context
RFC-007 keeps `doc_search` as long-tail retrieval and `corp_db_search` as hot-path retrieval. This only works if frequently used and stable documents can be promoted into `corp-db` through a deliberate pipeline.

Scope
- Define promotion candidate schema and manifest format.
- Build operator/report flow that selects candidates from usage stats.
- Implement ETL contract for turning promoted documents or chunks into corp-db-ingestible records.
- Define how promotion affects routing policy for short fact-like queries.
- Support re-promotion/re-ingest when source document hash changes.

Implementation notes
- Promotion is explicit and reviewable; no automatic silent promotion in v1.
- Keep chunking/normalization contracts simple and inspectable.
- Avoid tying promotion to one file path; source of truth should be CAS-backed document identity and manifest metadata.

Acceptance criteria
- Operator can generate promotion candidate list from real usage stats.
- Selected document can be transformed into corp-db-ready chunks/records.
- Updated source hash can trigger re-ingest path.
- Routing can prefer corp-db for promoted content without breaking fallback to doc_search.
close_reason: Implemented promotion candidate reports from usage stats and corp-db export manifests/JSONL for selected documents.

title: Implement deterministic company-fact fast path render and early stop
description: In core/agent.py, after the first relevant corp_db_search(kind=hybrid_search, profile=kb_search) for company facts, render the final answer deterministically in Python and terminate the run without extra LLM/tool iterations. Reuse existing renderer helpers where possible and keep scope limited to short company-fact questions.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic company-fact fast path render with early stop

title: Preserve benchmark algorithmic validation after runtime contract split
description: Verify and, if needed, adjust bench/bench_run.py, bench/bench_lib.py, and related evaluation helpers so algorithmic validation continues to consume metadata['bench_artifact'] or direct-tool payloads after runtime tool outputs become full-fidelity. The benchmark must not depend on compact runtime ToolResult.output for correctness or performance.
close_reason: Preserved algorithmic benchmark validation and added coverage that evaluation uses bench artifacts instead of runtime answer shape

title: RFC-025 review: revalidate loaded runtime route catalogs
description: Review P1. When an on-disk runtime route catalog exists at catalog.v1.json, load_routing_index currently accepts the raw _load_catalog_file payload if its embedded validation_report says valid. That can use stale validation metadata after code/schema changes, and _load_catalog_file can normalize/drop routes while preserving old report fields. Implement revalidation by rebuilding merged catalog metadata from the loaded runtime payload before it is treated as active. Acceptance: loaded runtime catalogs have current route_count, route_count_by_kind, source_manifests/source_digests and validation_report recomputed by current code; stale embedded validation_report cannot make a dropped/invalid route set active; regression tests cover a stale runtime catalog.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Harden degraded document route selection
description: Problem: degraded select_route() can still choose an unrelated doc_domain route for explicit document queries when no concrete document route matches, for example a fire certificate query can select the sports lighting norms document. Scope: change deterministic/degraded route ordering so document_lookup only selects doc_domain routes with a concrete route match; if no concrete document route matches, do not select an unrelated doc_domain route. Preserve LLM selector payload behavior for production and concrete document routes. Add/adjust tests for fire certificate without a matching live document route and sports norms with matching route wording. Acceptance: select_route('Найди пожарный сертификат LINE...') does not select doc_search.sports_lighting_norms unless a matching fire certificate document route exists; existing sports norms route tests still pass.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Variant 2: add ASR observability and credential protection on current path
close_reason: Canceled at user request; ASR is currently working well

title: Add runtime-vs-bench output policy helpers and guardrails
description: Introduce a small shared policy/helper layer for tool executors so benchmark artifact builders cannot accidentally become runtime payload formatters. The implementation should make the separation explicit in code review and easy to reuse by future tools. Include naming cleanup where current helper names encourage mixing compact runtime and artifact logic.
close_reason: Added shared tool_output_policy helpers and wired corp_db/doc_search to explicit runtime-vs-bench contracts

title: doc_search runtime: switch core to sidecar-based read path
description:
Context
RFC-010 treats `core` as a read-only retrieval layer over normalized sidecars instead of a parser runtime.

Problem
Current `doc_search` still reads and parses raw documents inside `core`, including optional `lit` fallback and format-specific extractors.

Scope
- Refactor `doc_search` to use normalized sidecars as the primary search substrate.
- Remove raw-file parsing from the normal hot path.
- Keep result shaping, scoring, snippets, usage stats, and guardrails in `core`.
- Define a degraded behavior when sidecars are missing or stale (`normalization_missing` or similar) rather than reparsing raw files in chat path.

Implementation notes
- Production-critical read path should use the minimal toolchain: `fd`, `rg`, `jq`.
- Read path must remain usable in read-only environments.
- Preserve backward-compatible output fields where reasonable.

Acceptance criteria
- A normal `doc_search` request does not parse raw binary documents in `core`.
- Retrieval works from normalized sidecars and manifests only.
- Missing sidecars do not trigger heavy reparsing on the chat path.
- Existing routing and observability continue to function.
close_reason: Switched core doc_search to normalized sidecar read path with degraded normalization_missing behavior.

title: RFC-027 follow-up: finish pure route-tree selector contract and family-local fallback policy
close_reason: Implemented

title: Polish agent-web pending state and iframe sizing
description: Follow-up UX polish for the embedded agent-web widget after the initial chrome cleanup. Scope: replace the remaining English pending-state placeholder with concise Russian user-facing copy, remove viewport-coupled layout rules that make the widget exceed its iframe bounds, and verify that the final widget fills the iframe cleanly with scrolling limited to the message list rather than the whole page. The goal is a production-ready embedded chat surface that matches the host site's language and behaves like a true iframe-sized widget.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Harden exact numeric equality filters in lamp_filters
description:
Validation after RFC-004 showed that direct hybrid_search requests using exact numeric equality style filters can still return false empty results, even when a realistic range-style request finds the correct lamp.

Observed case:
- beam_pattern=60° + dimensions_raw=774 x 428 x 406 мм + weight_kg_min=18.3 + weight_kg_max=18.3 returned empty
- the equivalent softer request with weight_kg_max=18.3 returned the expected R500-9-60 lamp

Required work:
- investigate exact numeric comparison behavior for lamp_filters and hybrid_search lamp filtering
- check normalization/rounding across numeric(10,3) catalog fields and request parsing
- make exact-match numeric filters no more fragile than bounded range filters for values such as weight_kg, dimensions, power_factor_min, and dimension ranges
- add regression tests for exact equality and mixed exact+text filters
- document any intended tolerance/normalization policy in code comments or skill examples if needed

Acceptance criteria:
- exact equality style numeric filters work for known catalog cases that currently reproduce false empty results
- tests cover at least weight_kg and one other numeric characteristic
- no recall regression for existing range-filter behavior
close_reason: Hardened decimal equality filters with range tolerance and added regression coverage

title: Tighten company-fact payload relevance and guardrail success conditions
description: Refine company-fact success detection so corp_db company-fact success is only marked when the payload is relevant to the requested fact type. Contacts require extracted contact fields or contact-specific headings; about-company requires company-profile headings; website/year queries require extracted values. Ensure guardrail does not block fallback on weak payloads.
close_reason: Tightened company-fact payload relevance checks and guardrail success conditions

title: Post-RFC-027 cleanup: classify and resolve failing routing_guardrail expectations
description:
## Context
RFC-027 implementation work is largely present in code/tests/docs, and the focused RFC-027 regression suite is green. However, the broader guardrail suite still shows unresolved drift:

```bash
python3 -m unittest -q core.tests.test_routing_guardrail
```

Current state: 54 tests run, 28 failures.

This creates a mismatch between closed RFC-027 remediation epics/issues and the current repository reality.

## Problem
We need a dedicated cleanup pass for the failing `core.tests.test_routing_guardrail` suite to separate:
1. obsolete expectations that still assert pre-RFC-027 behavior;
2. real regressions/integration gaps introduced or left behind by the RFC-027 rollout.

Without this split, it is unclear which failures should be fixed in code, which should be updated in tests, and which need follow-up issues.

## Scope
1. Run and audit all 28 failing `core.tests.test_routing_guardrail` cases.
2. Classify each failure into one of:
   - obsolete expectation under the final RFC-027 contract;
   - real regression / integration bug;
   - ambiguous case requiring explicit product decision.
3. For obsolete expectations:
   - update or remove tests with rationale tied to RFC-027 and follow-up remediation decisions.
4. For real regressions:
   - fix the code where the behavior is supposed to remain supported;
   - or split focused child issues if the fix is too large for one pass.
5. Reconcile BR/RFC status notes so closed epics do not overstate repo reality.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every currently failing `core.tests.test_routing_guardrail` test is triaged and classified.
- Obsolete expectations are updated/removed with an explicit rationale.
- Real regressions are either fixed or split into follow-up issues with clear ownership.
- `core.tests.test_routing_guardrail` is green, or any intentionally deferred failures are tracked explicitly in BR.
- Repo/test reality and RFC-027 closure notes are aligned.
close_reason: Triaged RFC-027 guardrail drift, aligned legacy expectations with selector-primary/LLM-only contract, added cleanup notes, and restored core.tests.test_routing_guardrail to green.

title: doc intake: repo inbox and sync-repo workflow
description:
Context
RFC-010 defines a simple admin-only repo workflow: documents are added to `doc-corpus/inbox/` and ingested by `doc-worker sync-repo`.

Problem
Current intake exists only as ad hoc CLI over arbitrary paths. There is no canonical repo inbox, no sidecar metadata convention, and no reproducible operator flow.

Scope
- Add `doc-corpus/inbox/` and `doc-corpus/manifests/` conventions.
- Extend the document ingestion CLI with `sync-repo`.
- Support optional `<filename>.meta.json` sidecars.
- Produce a structured report for ingested, duplicate, and rejected files.
- Keep the workflow admin-only and repo-driven.

Implementation notes
- The repo inbox is the source of truth for initial operator uploads.
- Runtime artifacts must still land in `/data/corp_docs/...`, not remain in the repo inbox.
- Rejected items must preserve machine-readable reasons.

Acceptance criteria
- Admin can copy a file to `doc-corpus/inbox/` and run one documented command.
- The command ingests all eligible repo inbox files into runtime storage.
- Duplicate and rejected outcomes are reported explicitly.
- Optional sidecar metadata is merged into manifests.
close_reason: Added repo inbox workflow, sync-repo CLI, metadata sidecars, and explicit ingest outcome reports.

title: RFC-025: define route card schema and validation contract
description:
Define the shared route-card schema used by runtime, generated catalogs, tests, and documentation.

Scope:

- Define how executor_args_template, locked_args, defaults, and LLM-produced tool_args are merged.
- Validate that tool_args contain only schema-declared fields and satisfy type, enum, bounds, pattern, maxLength, maxItems, and required constraints.
- Enforce compact enum policy: roughly 50-60 short values are acceptable; large domains like 700+ SKU must use free string/resolver style fields.
- Define one-shot repair behavior for invalid selector JSON/args.
- Reject unsafe selector output that attempts to override locked args, choose hidden routes, add undeclared paths/SQL/shell/tool names, or bypass evidence policy.

Acceptance:
- Route schema is documented and enforced by tests.
- Invalid LLM tool_args are rejected or repaired before any tool execution.

- Invalid selector output cannot fall back to score-based production routing.

title: RFC-025: implement merged route catalog lifecycle
description:
Build the explicit route catalog publication lifecycle required by RFC-025.

Scope:

- Emit one runtime-consumed catalog with schema_version, catalog_version, generated_at, source digests/manifests, route counts by route_kind, and validation report.
- Enforce unique route ids and explicit override rules.
- Ensure generated source-owned routes take precedence over bootstrap routes.
- Make production runtime health fail or return temporary-unavailable when no valid merged catalog exists.

Acceptance:
- Runtime has exactly one active merged route catalog.
- Duplicate route ids from different owners fail validation unless explicitly overridden.
- Every production route has executor, locked_args, argument_schema, and evidence_policy.
- Every doc_domain route has concrete document_selectors.
- Catalog validation reports missing coverage for known corp DB domains.

title: RFC-025: publish source-owned corp DB route cards
description:
Move corporate DB retrieval domains into source-owned route cards instead of scattered hard-coded routing lists.

Scope:

- Add route cards for missing structured domains named in the analysis: categories, ETM/ORACL SKU lookup, lamp_mountings/category mountings, mounting_types, lamp/category mounting compatibility.
- Model routes that span multiple tables when the retrieval workflow requires it.

Acceptance:

- Route cards define executor, locked DB scope, argument schema, hints, and evidence policy.

title: RFC-025: remodel doc_search as concrete document-domain routes
description:
Replace generic document lookup semantics with concrete document-domain route cards.

Scope:

- Require document_selectors or preferred_document_ids for every primary doc_domain route.
- Support multiple thematic routes for one document with shared selectors but different topics/summaries/hints/section scopes.
- Support one route over multiple concrete documents when the domain is explicitly defined.

Acceptance:

close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-025: add compact LLM selector for route and argument selection
description:
Implement the production selector described in RFC-025: one compact LLM call receives the user query and compact route cards, then returns strict JSON with the selected route and tool_args.

Scope:
- Build selector input from active route catalog: route id, title, summary, topics, keywords, executor, locked args, argument schema, and argument hints.
- If catalog is large, use only simple deterministic prefiltering to reduce candidates: exact ids, phrase matches, token overlap, and source availability.
- Require strict JSON output with selected_route_id, confidence, reason, tool_args, and optional fallback_route_ids.
- Validate output with the route schema and selector security contract before tool execution.
- On selector LLM outage, return temporary-unavailable rather than score-based fallback routing.

Acceptance:
- Selector chooses both route and tool_args.
- Selector output is traceable with confidence, reason, candidate ids, catalog version, and validated arg shape.
- Hand-weighted scoring is not used as production authority for route choice.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-025: implement route evidence policies and bounded fallback state machine
description:
Implement the runtime retrieval state machine and evidence classification from RFC-025.

Scope:
- Execute selected route executor with merged locked/default/validated tool args.
- Classify result as sufficient, weak, empty, or error through the route evidence_policy.
- For corp_db_search, require scope alignment: knowledge_route_id, source_files, topic_facets, result count, and relevant content.
- For doc_search, require document selector alignment, threshold, route-relevant matches, and active route family.
- Run at most one route-aware fallback: card fallback_route_ids, selector fallback_route_ids, then compatible prefilter candidate.
- Do not use generic doc_search fallback for company facts.
- Stop tool exploration once scoped evidence is sufficient.
- On finalizer LLM outage, return temporary-unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Non-empty wrong-route output is weak, not sufficient.
- Fallback is bounded to at most one route-aware attempt.
- Sufficient evidence proceeds directly to finalizer without extra irrelevant LLM/tool loops.
- The two production failure cases complete with selector + fast DB retrieval + final answer.

title: RFC-025: remove duplicated hard-coded routing logic from runtime

title: document corpus security: block search_files bypass and align guardrails
description:
Context
The new document corpus boundary blocks direct `/data/corp_docs` access for `read_file`, `list_directory`, and `search_text`, and routing guardrails treat document-path tool usage as a blocked wiki/doc path after successful corp-db company-fact answers. However `search_files` still does not enforce path safety at all.

Problem
- `tool_search_files()` does not call `is_path_safe()` and can be aimed at absolute glob patterns outside workspace.
- An agent can still enumerate `/data/corp_docs/**` or other managed roots through globbing.
- Guardrail detection in `core/agent.py` does not classify `search_files` as a document-path attempt, so the wiki/doc guardrail can still be bypassed.

Scope
- Add path-safety enforcement to `search_files`.
- Treat `/data/corp_docs` and legacy wiki roots as blocked managed corpus roots consistently across all generic file tools.
- Update routing guardrail detection to include `search_files`.
- Add tests proving both direct access and guardrail bypass are prevented.

Implementation notes
- Preserve existing workspace globbing behavior.
- Avoid reintroducing shell-based path expansion as a workaround.
- Keep error messages consistent with existing file-tool denial semantics.

Acceptance criteria
- `search_files` cannot enumerate `/data/corp_docs` or legacy document roots from agent sessions.
- Guardrail blocks `search_files` after successful company-fact `corp_db_search` unless document context was explicitly requested.
- Regression tests cover both generic file-tool denial and routing behavior.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Fix company-fact finalization and observability after corp_db errors
description:
Follow-up from RCA totosha-d3n:
- core/tools/corp_db.py returns ToolResult(success=True) even when payload status=error.
- core metrics therefore count corp_db_search as tool_status=success while retrieval payload is actually failing.
- agent can continue into LLM finalization and emit false-negative text like "корпоративная база временно недоступна" instead of deterministic fallback/result handling.

Scope:
- Treat payload status=error as tool failure or explicit failed retrieval state.
- Ensure company-fact path uses deterministic render/controlled fallback when corp_db succeeds.
- Improve metrics/meta so corp_db payload errors are visible as errors, not successes.

Acceptance:
- API meta for failed corp_db payload reports tool error state clearly.
- company-fact happy path avoids unnecessary extra LLM loops.
- regression test covers reproduced contacts scenario.

title: RFC-007: canonical doc-search naming and routing migration
description:
Context
RFC-007 вводит canonical naming `doc_search` / `doc-search`, оставляя `corp_wiki_search` и `corp-wiki-md-search` как deprecated aliases на переходный период. Сейчас prompt, skills, tests и tool registry всё ещё завязаны на старое wiki-oriented naming.

Scope

- Ввести canonical skill `doc-search` с multiformat document semantics.
- Сохранить backward-compatible aliases `corp_wiki_search` и `corp-wiki-md-search`.
- Обновить system prompt, skill descriptions, tool descriptions и routing examples так, чтобы agent выбирал между `corp_db_search` и `doc_search`.
- Обновить observability field naming только там, где это не ломает совместимость существующих bench results и dashboards.

Implementation notes
- Миграция должна быть additive-first: сначала aliases и dual-registration, потом switch prompt/tests, потом deprecation notes.
- Не ломать existing users of `corp_wiki_search` during rollout.
- В descriptions убрать markdown-only и wiki-only framing.

Acceptance criteria
- Agent prompt и skills используют `doc_search` как canonical path.
- `corp_wiki_search` остаётся рабочим alias.
- Existing tests can be updated without breaking backward compatibility.
- Naming in code and docs больше не вводит в заблуждение, что search ограничен wiki corpus.

title: Restore non-lossy corp_db_search contract in core
description:
Review showed that core/tools/corp_db.py now rewrites successful corp_db_search responses into a short human-readable summary. This is unsafe for production because it truncates results and drops fields that the agent may need to answer correctly for non-lamp or multi-row flows.

Problem:
- _format_result_summary emits only the first 5 rows and only up to 6 facts per row.
- It ignores structured fields from kinds such as sku_by_code and category_mountings.
- This is a regression versus the previous pretty-printed JSON tool output and can cause silent answer-quality regressions.

Required work:

Acceptance criteria:

title: promotion pipeline: consume normalized document exports in corp-db
description:

Acceptance criteria
