title: rollout: RFC-016..020 authoritative routing, doc domains, and observability
description:
Goal
Roll out the architecture and runtime changes defined in RFC-016..RFC-020 as one coordinated program.

Why
The current agent still mixes retrieval completion with answer rendering, routes too much logic through coarse heuristics, does not treat `doc_search` as a first-class domain, and lacks unified end-to-end observability for RCA.

Scope
- Implement authoritative route closure while keeping LLM finalization as the normal happy-path.
- Introduce source-scoped KB route families for company/common info, Luxnet, and lighting norms.
- Replace the current built-in routing subset with a unified routing catalog for table, script, and document domains.
- Restore the `doc-worker` write path and verify the inbox sports-lighting document as a real `doc_search` domain.
- Enable unified Victoria Metrics, Logs, and Traces correlation with shared request/route/tool identifiers.

Ordering
1. Fix retrieval closure and route semantics in the agent.
2. Make corporate KB routing source-scoped and safe from lamp-filter pollution.
3. Publish a unified routing catalog.
4. Restore and verify document-domain ingestion and retrieval.
5. Wire and validate observability against the new routing/runtime states.

Done when
- All child tasks are completed.
- Runtime behavior matches RFC-016..RFC-020.
- New routing and observability states are testable and observable in production-like runs.
close_reason: Rollout tasks completed

title: quality gates: tests, doctor, and observability for RFC-010 flows
description:
Context

Acceptance criteria

title: observability: add unified Victoria correlation fields and fresh-telemetry smoke
description:
Context
RFC-020 is not satisfied by transport-level traces alone. Operators need one correlated view across Victoria Metrics, Logs, and Traces with route and tool identifiers.

Problem
Even with OTEL enabled, RCA stays weak if logs, spans, and metrics do not share the same request/route/tool correlation fields and if there is no smoke test for fresh telemetry.

Scope
- Add structured correlation fields covering request, route, retrieval phase, and tool execution.
- Include identifiers such as `request_id`, `trace_id`, `selected_route_id`, `selected_route_family`, `tool_name`, `knowledge_route_id`, and `document_id` where applicable.
- Surface route closure, fallback, and guardrail-block events in logs and spans.
- Add smoke checks that prove one synthetic request appears in Victoria Metrics, Logs, and Traces simultaneously.
- Cover at least one KB-route request and one document-domain request in smoke validation.

Acceptance criteria
- Operators can reconstruct one request across metrics, logs, and traces using shared ids.
- Logs and spans include route-selection and tool-execution identifiers.
- Smoke fails when fresh traces/logs are missing even if metrics are still present.
- At least one document-domain query is included in the observability validation flow.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-027 follow-up: complete codes_and_sku schema for concrete code modes and reverse lookup
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-021 follow-up phase 5: add regression coverage for shortlist plus skills

title: routing catalog: publish unified route manifest for corp_table, corp_script, and doc_domain
description:
Context
RFC-018 replaces the current partial, built-in routing subset with a unified routing catalog that represents table routes, script routes, and document domains with one schema.

Problem
Routing logic is still split across prompt text, ad hoc heuristics, `core/agent.py`, and a small built-in route-card list. This makes route selection hard to explain and hard to evolve.

Scope
- Define a versioned routing-catalog schema.
- Represent `corp_table`, `corp_script`, and `doc_domain` routes with one common model.
- Publish merged route manifests that can be loaded by runtime as the primary source of truth.
- Keep only a minimal bootstrap catalog in code for empty-index fallback.
- Add route-selection telemetry including candidates, selected route, score, and selection reason.
- Align the catalog with RFC-017 source-scoped KB route families.

Acceptance criteria
- Runtime route selection uses a published merged catalog, not just hard-coded defaults.
- Catalog contains table, script, and document-domain routes.
- Operators can inspect which route was selected and why without reading prompt logic.
- Bootstrap defaults remain only as fallback when no published catalog exists.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Post-incident remediation: RFC-026 routing completion, corp-db migration, and ASR stability
description:
Incident remediation epic for the 2026-04-26/27 production degradation where the assistant failed many user questions.

Observed evidence:
- Trace `3a2eddb1208e49e0753268a5469b07e7`: `application_recommendation` failed because live Postgres lacks `corp.sphere_curated_categories`, then runtime fell into an invalid `hybrid_search` fallback and `tools-api` returned `422`.
- Trace `f23debb700469612b9c97b546bc61095` and `edb59c909c46d538cc91b235efff65f2`: series questions routed through `corp_db.catalog_lookup`, first hit `lamp_exact`, then an invalid `knowledge_route_id=corp_db.catalog_lookup`, and finally answered from a model card instead of a series-level result.
- Voice path had 6 `403` responses on `/transcribe` in 1h. Error artifacts in the CLIProxyAPI fork show Cloudflare challenge HTML from `chatgpt.com/backend-api/transcribe`.
- One LLM request failed with `proxy` `502` / `Cannot write to closing transport` and the user got `(нет ответа)`.
- p95 latency for `core /api/chat` in the same window was ~28.5s.

RFC review:
- Read RFC-026 (`Canonical Route Arguments, Curated Sphere Categories, And Bounded Portfolio Retrieval`).
- Large parts were implemented in-repo: route-card enums, curated sphere/category data model, selector/runtime sphere context, tests.
- The incident shows rollout and runtime behavior are still incomplete: no idempotent live DB migration, incomplete series semantics, and bounded failure policy not fully enforced after application-route errors.

This epic tracks the concrete bugs/tasks needed to close the gap between the RFC and production behavior.
close_reason: Implemented via child issues totosha-pfit.1-.7

title: Add idempotent live migration for RFC-026 corp-db schema (sphere_curated_categories, parent_category_id)
description:
Problem
- RFC-026 data-model work exists in repo (`db/init.sql`, `db/catalog_loader.py`, `db/spheres.json`) but the live DB volume was not migrated.
- Verified directly in the running container:
  - `to_regclass('corp.sphere_curated_categories')` -> `NULL`
  - `corp.categories.parent_category_id` column is also absent.
- As a result, `application_recommendation` crashes on live traffic with `relation "corp.sphere_curated_categories" does not exist`.

Why this matters
- This is the primary root cause for recommendation failures such as `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?`.
- It also means RFC-026 is only "implemented on empty bootstrap" and not operationally rolled out for existing persistent volumes.

Requested work
1. Add an idempotent migration path for existing corp-db volumes:
   - create `corp.sphere_curated_categories` if missing;
   - add `corp.categories.parent_category_id` if missing;
   - create required indexes/constraints safely;
   - seed curated sphere/category edges from `db/spheres.json`.
2. Ensure startup/bootstrap can apply these schema upgrades outside of first-init only.
3. Add a health/doctor check that fails when RFC-026-required schema objects are missing.
4. Add an operator-visible remediation/runbook section for upgrading existing DBs.
5. Add regression tests covering "existing DB without new tables/columns" upgrade path.

Acceptance criteria
- A running pre-RFC-026 volume upgrades without manual table-by-table SQL.
- `application_recommendation` no longer errors with missing relation on upgraded environments.
- `doctor.py` or equivalent health check explicitly reports missing RFC-026 schema drift.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Complete RFC-026 routing for series/category asks and remove invalid corp_db.catalog_lookup fallback semantics
description:
Problem
- RFC-026 is only partially realized in runtime routing behavior.
- Production traces for series questions (`f23debb700469612b9c97b546bc61095`, `edb59c909c46d538cc91b235efff65f2`) show:
  1. route selection lands on `corp_db.catalog_lookup`;
  2. first tool call is `lamp_exact`;
  3. later runtime attempts `hybrid_search` with invalid `knowledge_route_id=corp_db.catalog_lookup` (`400 Unknown knowledge_route_id`);
  4. the final answer is built from a single lamp card instead of a series-level answer.
- In code, RFC-026 artifacts exist, but the "series semantics" part is incomplete:
  - `SERIES_AWARE_ROUTE_IDS` currently covers only `category_mountings` and `lamp_mounting_compatibility`;
  - `corp_db.catalog_lookup` still conflates exact model lookup with broad series/category asks;
  - application-routing improvements from RFC-026 are not fully reflected in route-card boundaries for series asks.

Requested work
1. Revisit RFC-026 route boundaries and make series-level asks first-class runtime behavior.
   - either add a dedicated series-aware route,
   - or split/refine `catalog_lookup` so broad `серии` requests do not start with `lamp_exact`.
2. Prevent runtime from ever synthesizing `knowledge_route_id=corp_db.catalog_lookup` for `corp_db_search`; that route id is a route-card id, not a valid corp-db knowledge route.
3. Review route-card argument allowlists and fallback logic so broad series/category asks cannot degrade into exact-model lookup by default.
4. Add replay/integration tests for:
   - `какие у вас есть серии светильников?`
   - `в общей базе есть описание всех серий`
   - other broad series-family asks
5. Confirm routing telemetry makes the chosen series/category path explainable from traces.

Acceptance criteria
- Broad series questions no longer start with `lamp_exact` unless the user actually asks about an exact model.
- No runtime path emits `knowledge_route_id=corp_db.catalog_lookup`.
- Series answers are derived from series-aware evidence, not from an arbitrary single lamp card.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Bound application_recommendation recovery and prevent invalid corp_db_search arg mixes after primary failure
description:
Problem
- Trace `3a2eddb1208e49e0753268a5469b07e7` shows that after `application_recommendation` fails, runtime continues into an open-ended fallback path and emits:
  `corp_db_search(kind=hybrid_search, ..., limit_categories=0, limit_lamps=0, limit_portfolio=0)`
- `tools-api` correctly rejects this with `422`, because `limit_categories` and `limit_lamps` must be `>= 1`.
- RFC-026 explicitly requires bounded, domain-specific stop/fallback behavior instead of unrelated retrieval after empty/error states.
- `core/agent.py` already contains intent-specific fallback helper stubs, but the application-route failure policy is not actually closed/bounded in the same way as portfolio handling.

Requested work
1. Implement a bounded failure policy for `application_recommendation` consistent with RFC-026:
   - one canonicalization/clarification step at most;
   - no unrelated `hybrid_search`, `sphere_categories`, `lamp_filters`, or portfolio hops after application-route failure unless explicitly allowed by route policy.
2. Ensure core never sends application-only limit fields on non-application kinds.
3. Tighten route-argument validation / normalization so incompatible arg mixes are blocked before tool execution.
4. Add traceable observability fields for "bounded_application_fallback" vs "stopped_after_primary_error".
5. Add replay tests for `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?` covering both healthy-DB and degraded-DB behavior.

Acceptance criteria
- After application-route error/empty result, runtime performs at most one bounded recovery step.
- `corp_db_search(kind=hybrid_search, limit_categories=0, limit_lamps=0, ...)` is impossible.
- Degraded user-facing response is concise and deterministic instead of an extra invalid tool loop.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Harden CLIProxyAPI fork /transcribe path with per-credential challenge observability and cooldown
description:
Problem
- Voice failures are not caused by the local `/transcribe` routing itself; we intentionally run a special CLIProxyAPI fork so the ChatGPT-compatible `/transcribe` endpoint exists.
- The observed failures come after that compatibility path: challenge HTML from `chatgpt.com/backend-api/transcribe` is being returned as `403`.
- Current fork/error artifacts prove the path works mechanically, but operational hardening is incomplete:
  - artifacts show challenge HTML and downstream traceparent;
  - the transcribe failure artifact does not identify which auth file / credential was used;
  - repeated challenge-prone credentials are not quarantined from `/transcribe` traffic.
- This matches the open gaps in RFC-023.

Requested work
1. Keep the forked `/transcribe` compatibility path, but harden it per RFC-023:
   - classify challenge HTML as `upstream_challenge`;
   - log backend mode and credential/auth-file id for `/transcribe` requests;
   - add counters for success/failure/challenge by backend + credential;
   - add temporary cooldown/quarantine for credentials repeatedly challenged on `/transcribe`.
2. Surface these signals in operator diagnostics / admin health.
3. Ensure bot-side UX keeps the normalized temporary-error message and never leaks raw HTML.
4. Document the current fork contract (`feature/chatgpt-transcribe-endpoint` / `../CLIProxyAPI-fork`) and the difference between "compatibility path works" vs "upstream challenge is blocking it".
5. Evaluate whether production default should remain ChatGPT-compatible at all, or whether stable OpenAI-compatible/faster-whisper should become the default with chatgpt-compat as fallback-only.

Acceptance criteria
- Each `/transcribe` failure can be tied to a concrete auth file / credential id.
- Repeated challenge failures on one credential stop reusing that credential for a cooldown window.
- Operators can distinguish "fork path broken" from "upstream challenged a working forked path".
close_reason: Implemented (verified manually after Codex session exited without closing issue)

title: Investigate and harden proxy/CLIProxyAPI handling of upstream disconnects (502 Cannot write to closing transport)
description:
Problem
- One user-facing request failed completely with `proxy` `502` and `LLM proxy error: Cannot write to closing transport`.
- This produced `(нет ответа)` on the bot side.
- The same observation window had high tail latency across the LLM chain, so transport lifecycle handling under long requests needs hardening.

Requested work
1. Reproduce and investigate the `Cannot write to closing transport` failure path in `proxy` and/or CLIProxyAPI upstream interaction.
2. Determine whether the fix belongs in:
   - `proxy` streaming/lifecycle handling,
   - upstream request timeout/cancellation settings,
   - CLIProxyAPI request semantics,
   - or retry policy for safe idempotent LLM calls.
3. Define a safe retry/degradation policy so one upstream disconnect does not produce an empty bot response.
4. Add observability that correlates 5xx proxy failures with upstream request ids / connection lifecycle.
5. Add a regression/integration test for abrupt upstream close during streaming or response write.

Acceptance criteria
- Reproduced root cause is documented.
- The common upstream-close case no longer surfaces as `(нет ответа)` without a controlled fallback/user message.
- Proxy 5xx events are traceable to an actionable upstream cause.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Fix ineffective context trimming and make oversized-chat latency budget enforceable
description:
Problem
- In the same incident window, `core /api/chat` p95 was ~28.5s.
- Logs show: `Context too large (44121 chars > 40000), trimming...` immediately followed by `Trimmed context to 44121 chars`.
- This indicates the trimming logic can exit without actually bringing context below the configured cap, which amplifies latency and increases the chance of long multi-iteration failures.

Requested work
1. Fix context trimming so the post-trim payload is guaranteed to be <= configured budget or the request fails/degrades explicitly.
2. Review whether the current retained message strategy keeps too much irrelevant tool output / conversation history.
3. Add observability for pre-trim size, post-trim size, number of removed messages, and whether trimming hit a hard stop.
4. Add tests for pathological cases where only a few huge middle messages remain.
5. Re-evaluate latency budgets for route-selector + finalizer + tool loops after trimming is fixed.

Acceptance criteria
- The log line `Trimmed context to X` always reports `X <= MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS`.
- Oversized contexts no longer silently continue above budget.
- Tail latency improves or at least becomes attributable via new metrics/log fields.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Add replay checks, synthetic smoke tests, and alerts for the routing/ASR incident paths
description:
Create the verification layer for this incident class.

Scope
- Replay the concrete failing traces/questions that exposed the bugs.
- Add synthetic checks/alerts so services cannot stay "up=1" while key user paths are broken.

Must cover
1. Corp DB / routing replays:
   - `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?`
   - `какие у вас есть серии светильников?`
   - `в общей базе есть описание всех серий`
2. ASR chain:
   - smoke for `bot -> proxy /transcribe -> CLIProxyAPI fork /transcribe`
   - challenge/error-rate alerts
3. DB schema drift checks for RFC-026-required objects.
4. Proxy 5xx alerts and tail-latency SLO-style views.

Dependencies
- This issue should be completed after the remediation bugs land so the replays encode the corrected behavior.

Acceptance criteria
- Synthetic checks fail if the curated schema is missing, ASR challenge rate spikes, or the known routing queries regress.
- Operator dashboards/alerts make these failure modes visible without manual trace archaeology.
close_reason: Implemented

title: agent loop: close authoritative retrieval before LLM finalization
description:
Context
RFC-016 defines the main runtime defect: the agent may keep the tool loop open even after authoritative retrieval already returned sufficient evidence.

Problem
A successful `corp_db_search` currently does not always close retrieval. The model may continue to call more tools, which increases latency and can produce contradictory answers.

Scope
- Introduce explicit retrieval-phase state separate from answer rendering state.
- Add canonical fields such as `retrieval_phase`, `retrieval_evidence_status`, `retrieval_retry_count`, and `retrieval_close_reason`.
- Close the route when route-specific sufficiency checks pass.
- Keep `finalizer_mode=llm` as the normal happy-path.
- Block new tool calls after retrieval closure unless policy explicitly allows post-answer enrichment.
- Demote or replace misleading compatibility-only fields such as `company_fact_fast_path` and `company_fact_rendered`.
- Add regression coverage for primary attempt, route-local retry, and post-closure guardrail behavior.

Acceptance criteria
- After `success+sufficient`, no additional tool call happens for the same base question.
- If the first attempt is `weak` but the second in the same route is sufficient, retrieval closes and no further tools run.
- The final user answer still goes through the LLM in the normal path.
- Guardrail blocks any extra tool call after closure and logs the block reason.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-007: parse cache keyed by CAS content hash
description:
Context
RFC-007 требует, чтобы expensive parsing and OCR were reusable across duplicate documents and repeated queries. Cache должен быть привязан к content hash, а не к пути файла.

Scope
- Ввести normalized sidecars (`text.txt`, `structured.json`, `meta.json`) for parsed documents.
- Key cache primarily by `sha256(file contents)` from CAS.
- Use file size / mtime only as fast invalidation hints before hash verification where relevant.
- Make cache reusable across multiple logical aliases pointing to one CAS blob.
- Add cache invalidation/versioning strategy based on parser version and OCR config hash.

Implementation notes
- Cache lives in `/data/corp_docs/cache/`.
- Avoid duplicating parsed artifacts for the same blob.
- Keep schema simple and inspectable.

Acceptance criteria
- Same CAS blob reuses one parse cache.
- Cache invalidates when parser version or OCR config changes.
- `doc_search` reports whether result came from cache.
- Repeated query on already parsed document avoids full re-parse.
close_reason: Implemented parse cache keyed by CAS sha256 with versioned cache directories and cache-hit reporting.

title: doc_search observability: preserve metrics/stats parity on corp_wiki_search alias
description:
Context
During migration the deprecated alias `corp_wiki_search` still remains callable. At the moment the alias reimplements a minimal wrapper over `search_documents()` and does not go through the canonical `tool_doc_search()` path.

Problem
- Alias requests do not emit the same metrics, spans, and usage-stat records as canonical `doc_search`.
- This creates blind spots exactly during the migration period when some traffic may still use the old name.
- Promotion analytics and debugging become inconsistent depending on which alias the agent selected.

Scope
- Make deprecated alias execution reuse the same observability and usage-stat path as canonical `doc_search`, or otherwise emit equivalent telemetry.
- Preserve alias-specific metadata only where needed for compatibility/debugging.
- Add tests covering alias parity for tool output metadata and usage logging.

Implementation notes
- Prefer a thin alias that delegates to the canonical implementation rather than a second partial implementation.
- Keep backward-compatible output fields where required, but do not fork the instrumentation path.

Acceptance criteria
- `corp_wiki_search` and `doc_search` produce equivalent metrics/usage records for equivalent inputs.
- Migration traffic is visible in promotion analytics and debug telemetry.
- Tests verify observability parity or intentional alias-specific differences.
close_reason: Fixed

title: RFC-021 follow-up: simplify routing while preserving skills
close_reason: Implemented

title: Add tests for company-fact fast path and relevance checks
description: Add unit and integration coverage for company-fact subtype classification, canonical query rewrite, payload relevance validation, deterministic rendering, and guardrail behavior for contacts/about-company/company facts. Cover both positive and weak-payload cases.
close_reason: Added regression tests for subtype classification, rewrite, relevance, rendering, and route guardrails

title: RFC remediation: tighten doc-domain heuristics and restore corp-script route selection
description:
Problem
- RFC-018/RFC-019 expect document-domain routing to win only on true document signals, while RFC-017/RFC-018 expect corp-script routes to remain selectable for broad-object recommendation queries.
- Current `explicit_document_request` heuristics are too broad because the keyword list includes `ссылка`, which incorrectly sends normal KB questions like contacts/Luxnet page requests into `doc_search`.
- Current scoring also misses common inflected recommendation phrasing such as `агрессивной среды`, causing `application_recommendation` to fall below selection threshold.

Scope
- Refine document-request heuristics in `core/documents/routing.py` so generic `ссылка` by itself does not override authoritative KB routes.
- Keep strong document signals such as `документ`, `фрагмент`, `цитата`, certificate/passport/PDF requests, and explicit in-document phrasing.
- Improve corp-script route scoring for common recommendation/object phrases and inflected forms so representative application queries reliably select `corp_db.application_recommendation`.
- Add regression tests for both classes of failures.

Acceptance Criteria
- `select_route("Ссылка на контакты компании")` keeps the request on `corp_kb.company_common`.
- `select_route("Нужна ссылка на страницу Luxnet")` keeps the request on `corp_kb.luxnet`.
- `select_route("Какие светильники подходят для агрессивной среды?")` selects `corp_db.application_recommendation`.
- Existing routing catalog tests continue to pass and new routing regressions are covered by automated tests.

Files Likely Touched
- `core/documents/routing.py`
- `core/tests/test_routing_catalog.py`
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-026: migrate category hierarchy and curated sphere categories
description:
Goal
- Prepare source data and DB schema so runtime can store curated sphere categories separately from the full imported sphere-category relation.

Work
- Extend `db/spheres.json` with `curatedCategoryIds` for all 12 spheres using the approved mapping from RFC-026.
- Persist `parent_category_id` in `corp.categories` from `db/categories.json.parent.id`.
- Add new table `corp.sphere_curated_categories` with at least: `sphere_id`, `category_id`, `position`, `source_hash` and standard timestamps if required by local schema conventions.
- Update DB initialization/migration path and `db/catalog_loader.py` seeding so both relations are loaded:
  - `corp.sphere_categories` = full imported linkage
  - `corp.sphere_curated_categories` = curated runtime subset
- Define backfill and rebuild behavior for existing environments.

Acceptance notes
- Curated mapping contains 33 sphere-category edges.
- Parent linkage is queryable from DB.
- Existing full import behavior is preserved.

Dependencies
- None. This is the schema/data foundation for the rest of RFC-026.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Tolerate exact numeric equality filters in lamp_filters
description: Validation after RFC-004 showed that a direct hybrid_search request with weight_kg_min=18.3, weight_kg_max=18.3 and dimensions_raw for the R500-9-60 case returned empty, while the realistic agent-style request with weight_kg_max=18.3 returned the expected lamp. Investigate exact numeric equality handling and normalization in lamp_filters so exact-match numeric filters are not more fragile than range filters.
close_reason: Fixed by totosha-get exact numeric equality hardening

title: Fix corp_db rollout smoke and eval runbook for prod sign-off
description:
The current rollout runbook cannot produce a trustworthy sign-off because the RFC-004 smoke instructions generate a subset dataset in a tracked path and then evaluate results against the full golden dataset by default.

Problem:
- docs/operations/bench-runbook.md creates bench/golden/rfc004-smoke.jsonl inside the tracked golden directory
- the follow-up bench_eval invocation omits --dataset for the smoke subset
- this causes missing_result noise and a false fail during operator validation

Required work:
- update the rollout runbook so smoke subset generation uses a safe temporary or explicit path
- make the evaluation command explicitly reference the same subset dataset
- include a short operator checklist for interpreting upstream LLM 429 failures separately from search regressions
- ensure the documented workflow produces a valid pass/fail summary for the intended smoke scope
- optionally add a helper script if that materially reduces operator error

Acceptance criteria:
- the documented smoke/eval flow works as written on a subset dataset without false missing_result failures
- the runbook clearly distinguishes external LLM quota failures from corp_db/search regressions
- operators can use the documented commands to produce prod sign-off evidence
close_reason: Updated smoke runbook to use temp dataset, explicit --dataset eval, and LLM-429 triage guidance

title: Operations remediation: restore writable google-workspace-mcp credentials mount
description:
Problem
- `google-workspace-mcp` restarts because startup cannot create `/app/store_creds/.permission_test`.
- The host bind mount `workspace/_shared/google_creds` is owned by `root:root` with mode `755`, while the container runs as non-root user `app`, so OAuth token persistence is broken after deploy.
- We need a durable fix that preserves the non-root runtime and makes the credentials directory writable in the supported deployment path.

Scope
- Update the deployment/runtime setup for `google-workspace-mcp` so the credentials directory mounted at `/app/store_creds` is writable by the container user.
- Prefer a repo-local, reproducible fix over manual host-only chmod steps when possible.
- Verify by restarting `google-workspace-mcp` and checking that the permission-check error disappears and the container reaches healthy/running state.
- Document any operator requirement only if the final fix genuinely needs it.

Acceptance Criteria
- `docker compose up -d google-workspace-mcp` starts without the `.permission_test` permission error.
- The container keeps running under a non-root user or with an equally justified secure configuration.
- The mounted credentials path remains writable for OAuth token persistence across restarts.
- No unrelated Google Workspace feature changes are mixed into this issue.

Files Likely Touched
- `docker-compose.yml`
- `google-workspace-mcp/Dockerfile`
- `google-workspace-mcp/entrypoint.sh`
- docs only if required
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-027: wire family-aware prompting, observability, and replay coverage
description:
Complete the RFC-027 rollout in agent prompt construction, smoke coverage, and docs.

Scope:
- update route-selector prompting/compact payload to present family + leaf structure instead of a flat leaf pool
- update/extend tests for selector payloads, observability metadata, and route-specific behavior
- refresh RFC index / docs references if needed

Acceptance criteria:
- selector prompt path uses the family-first compact payload
- tests cover broad series, RZD sphere/category, mounting, document-by-lamp, and code-lookup route selection under the new hierarchy
- br state and docs remain in sync with implementation
close_reason: Implemented family-aware selector payload/prompting and regression coverage

title: doc-worker: restore build and operator runtime for inbox ingestion and legacy Office parsing
description:
Context
RFC-019 treats `doc_search` as a first-class domain, but the current `doc-worker` write path still needs to be restored and validated, especially for legacy Office input.

Problem
The repo inbox already contains a legacy `.doc` file for a sports-lighting domain, but `doc-worker` must reliably build and run before that domain can be published and searched.

Scope
- Fix the `doc-worker` build/runtime path so operator commands work again.
- Verify `sync-repo`, `rebuild-parsed`, and `rebuild-routes` end to end.
- Validate the legacy `.doc` normalization path through the guaranteed worker runtime.
- Ensure published artifacts land in the expected `/data/corp_docs` layout.
- Document any remaining limitations if a sub-step is intentionally deferred.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc-worker` builds and the documented operator commands complete successfully.
- The legacy `.doc` inbox file can be normalized through the supported worker path.
- Live manifest, parsed sidecar, and route artifacts are published in the runtime corpus layout.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC remediation: fix duplicate request correlation and propagate tool-call IDs to tools-api
description:
Problem
- RFC-020 requires one coherent correlation contract across request/route/tool spans, logs, and metrics.
- Current core API records retrieval correlation twice per `/api/chat`: once in HTTP middleware and again manually in the handler, which double-counts route metrics.
- Current `core -> tools-api` transport does not propagate `tool_call_id` / `tool_call_seq`, so downstream tool spans/logs cannot disambiguate multiple tool calls within one traced request.

Scope
- Remove duplicate request-correlation observation from `core/api.py` and rely on one consistent request-level metric path.
- Propagate tool call correlation headers from core tool executors into `tools-api` for `corp_db_search`.
- In `tools-api`, ingest those headers into correlation context before route execution so spans/logs/metrics include stable tool-call identifiers.
- Add regression coverage for request correlation behavior and tool-call propagation where practical.

Acceptance Criteria
- `observe_request_correlation` is not invoked twice for the same `/api/chat` request path.
- `tool_call_id` and `tool_call_seq` are available in `tools-api` correlation context for `corp_db_search` requests from core.
- Existing tests for core/tool transport continue to pass, with new coverage for propagated headers or correlation fields.
- No routing-selection logic changes are mixed into this issue.

Files Likely Touched
- `core/api.py`
- `core/tools/corp_db.py`
- `tools-api/src/routes/corp_db.py`
- observability tests if present
close_reason: Implemented

title: legacy cleanup: remove corp_wiki_search tool and corp-wiki-md-search skill alias
description:
Goal
Delete the deprecated runtime alias layer once canonical naming is fully adopted.

Why
Keeping both `doc_search` and `corp_wiki_search` registered in runtime/tooling leaves two names for one capability, increases routing ambiguity for the model, and expands the surface area that must be tested and maintained.

Scope
- Remove deprecated alias tool registration from core and tools-api.
- Remove the `core/tools/corp_wiki.py` alias wrapper once callers are migrated.
- Remove the deprecated `shared_skills/skills/corp-wiki-md-search` wrapper once no runtime path depends on it.
- Update docs and inventories so only canonical tool/skill names remain.
- Preserve a clear changelog/migration note for operators if needed.

Dependencies
- Should start only after prompts/tests/bench and legacy storage paths are migrated away from the alias.

Out of scope
- Reworking doc-search retrieval behavior itself.
- Historical RFC cleanup beyond small forward references.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` / `doc-search` are the only runtime-registered names for document retrieval.
- No active tool registry, skill registry, or API definition exposes `corp_wiki_search` or `corp-wiki-md-search`.
- Backward-compat references remain only in changelog/history where intentionally documented.
close_reason: Removed deprecated corp_wiki_search tool alias and corp-wiki-md-search skill/runtime files from core, tools-api, and tests.

title: RFC-004 corp_db search latency and observability hardening
description:
Implements RFC-004: speed up corp_db retrieval for filter-heavy catalog queries, remove empty timeout errors in core, and make Victoria metrics/traces/logs usable for end-to-end latency triage.

Scope:
- tools-api fast path for explicit lamp filters
- delayed embeddings and fallback pruning
- core-side timeout/error diagnostics
- observability buckets, phase metrics, and trace spans
- dashboards/runbook validation and smoke comparison
close_reason: RFC-004 implementation complete.

title: tools-api fast path for explicit lamp filters
description:
RFC-004 task 1. Rework hybrid_search so explicit lamp filters use authoritative lamp_filters as the first decision point instead of always paying for the full hybrid path.

Deliverables:
- call lamp_filters before expensive hybrid stages when explicit lamp filters are present
- return hybrid_search response immediately with a stable search_strategy when filter results are sufficient
- preserve current corp_db_search API contract and payload shape
- add tests proving token fallback is skipped when lamp_filters already returned valid results
close_reason: Implemented fast path for explicit lamp filters in hybrid_search with authoritative lamp_filters results and fallback skip tests.

title: observability: enable OTEL wiring and trace-context propagation by default
description:
Context
RFC-020 starts with one foundation requirement: the runtime must consistently start with OTEL wiring enabled and propagate trace context across internal service hops.

Problem
Metrics can look healthy while current traces and correlated logs remain absent if services are started without OTEL environment wiring or without `traceparent` propagation.

Scope
- Make OTEL wiring part of the default supported deployment path.
- Ensure production-critical services start with the required OTEL exporter configuration.
- Propagate `traceparent`, `tracestate`, and `X-Request-Id` across internal HTTP hops.
- Validate the wiring on the current bot/core/proxy/tools-api chain.
- Update deployment/runbook documentation so operators cannot accidentally skip the tracing path.

Acceptance criteria
- Fresh deployments of the runtime services include OTEL exporter configuration.
- A new request produces non-empty trace identifiers in current-service telemetry.
- Internal hops preserve distributed trace context end to end.
close_reason: Implemented

title: RFC-007: doc_search engine v2 with Unix-style execution graph
description:
Context
`doc_search` должен стать bounded multiformat retrieval tool. RFC-007 специально уходит от одного жёсткого pipeline и предлагает allowlisted execution graph вокруг `fd`, `rg`, `rga`, `ugrep`, `liteparse`, `jq`.

Scope
- Реализовать canonical `doc_search` tool executor.
- Добавить multi-root discovery (`legacy wiki root`, `document live root`).
- Добавить file type classification и backend selection policy.
- Поддержать allowlisted execution patterns, включая text-first search и file-scoped `liteparse` execution.
- Вернуть нормализованный structured result contract: document_id, relative_path, file_type, match_mode, snippet, page/sheet/slide, cache_hit, score.

Implementation notes
- Avoid agent-level shell composition; orchestration lives inside bounded executor.
- `liteparse` не должен быть universal first step.
- Prefer cheap adapters (`rg`/`rga`/`ugrep`) before parse-on-demand.
- Support stdin/stdout-friendly patterns where that simplifies reliable execution.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` can search at least md/pdf/docx/xlsx/pptx/png/jpg/tiff according to RFC-007.
- Tool returns structured payload instead of ad hoc markdown dump.
- Execution graph is allowlisted and observable.
- Existing alias `corp_wiki_search` can delegate to the new engine.
close_reason: Implemented doc_search engine v2 with multi-root discovery, multiformat extraction, legacy wiki compatibility, and structured results.

title: legacy cleanup: migrate off corp-wiki legacy corpus path
description:
Goal
Remove direct dependence on the legacy wiki storage path and make canonical doc-corpus ingestion the only supported source for searchable documents.

Why
`doc_search` still knows about the old `/data/skills/corp-wiki-md-search/wiki` location. That keeps a hidden second source of truth for documents, complicates operator workflows, and blocks complete removal of the legacy skill.

Scope
- Inventory any remaining reads from `/data/skills/corp-wiki-md-search/wiki` or equivalent legacy paths.
- Move remaining seed/example documents into the canonical doc-corpus / doc-worker intake flow.
- Remove fallback discovery logic tied to the legacy wiki directory.
- Update operator docs/runbooks so document availability is explained only through canonical inbox -> worker -> live flow.
- Add or update migration notes for existing deployments that still have documents in the legacy path.

Out of scope
- Changing CAS/sidecar formats.
- Removing prompt/test references unless directly required for migration docs.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` no longer reads from the legacy corp-wiki filesystem path.
- All supported searchable docs come from canonical manifests/sidecars under the doc-worker managed corpus.
- Operators have a documented migration path for legacy markdown/wiki content.
close_reason: doc_search now reads only managed corp_docs manifests/sidecars; seed docs moved into doc-corpus/inbox and operator migration docs updated.

title: prompts and skills: promote doc_search to a first-class document-domain route
description:
Context
RFC-019 requires `doc_search` to be described as its own route domain. Current prompt/skill guidance still tends to frame it as a fallback path too often.

Problem
Even after the document domain exists, the agent can continue to underuse it if prompt and skill contracts do not reflect first-class document-domain routing.

Scope
- Update prompt rules so `doc_search` is selected for explicit document-domain signals, not only after KB misses.
- Update skill descriptions and operator docs to match the new routing contract.
- Remove or narrow wording that implies `doc_search` is merely a fallback.
- Align route-policy language with the unified routing catalog from RFC-018.
- Add regression coverage for explicit document-domain requests.

Acceptance criteria
- Prompt and skill text describe `doc_search` as a first-class document-domain route.
- Explicit document queries prefer `doc_search` without requiring a prior KB miss.
- Updated docs and tests match the actual runtime behavior.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Remove stale generic doc_search route cards
description: Problem: doc_search.document_lookup is filtered out by core/documents/routing.py at runtime, but stale catch-all cards still exist in doc-corpus/manifests/routes/*.json, workspace/_shared/corp_docs/manifests/routes/*.json, and bootstrap_route_cards(). This is confusing and contradicts RFC-025 concrete document-domain semantics. Scope: remove the bootstrap doc_search.document_lookup card; remove or regenerate checked-in route manifests so doc_search.document_lookup and the old 'Document lookup and certificates' card are absent; keep concrete doc_domain routes such as sports_lighting_norms; keep runtime load_routing_index valid. Do not edit root-owned runtime files if not writable from host; ensure runtime revalidation still filters stale operational state. Acceptance: rg for doc_search.document_lookup returns only tests/RFC historical references if any; active load_routing_index has no doc_search.document_lookup; routing catalog tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented

title: update dashboards, runbook, and smoke latency validation for RFC-004
description:
RFC-004 task 6. Finish rollout by validating that the new search strategy and observability signals improve real requests and are documented for operators.

Deliverables:
- update Grafana dashboards and any alert queries affected by larger histogram buckets or new phase metrics
- update observability and bench triage runbooks with the new workflow
- run retrieval smoke subset and compare latency before/after RFC-004 changes
- capture acceptance evidence for pass rate, duration, and request_id-based triage
close_reason: Updated dashboard/runbooks/signal catalog, ran observability-harness verify, and validated RFC-004 behavior through Victoria stack. Bench subset run was blocked by upstream LLM 429s, so direct tools-api smoke was used for search-path acceptance.

title: Implement RFC-008 application-driven lighting recommendation fast path
description:
Source: docs/rfc/RFC-008-application-driven-lighting-recommendation-fast-path.md

Implement the RFC-008 broad-object recommendation path for catalog questions such as stadium, quarry, airport, warehouse, office, and aggressive-environment scenarios.

Scope:
- add deterministic application-driven retrieval path in corp_db_search
- return links, images, portfolio examples, and one clarifying follow-up question
- update prompt/skill routing so broad-object questions stop relying on generic hybrid_search
- add observability, tests, and benchmark coverage

Done when:
- all dependent tasks are completed
- benchmark cases derived from docs/questions.md are covered
- the new path is observable and can replace the ad hoc multi-step retrieval flow for supported application scenarios
close_reason: RFC-008 implementation completed across backend, routing, observability, tests, and benchmark

title: doc-worker foundation: add operator-side LiteParse service
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends integrating LiteParse through a dedicated operator-side worker instead of putting heavy parsing dependencies into the `core` hot path.

Problem
Today the project has no dedicated document worker service. `core` remains the only plausible place for parsing, which conflicts with the platform architecture and makes future `lit` integration heavier and less safe.

Scope
- Add a new operator-side `doc-worker` service and Docker image.
- Install the required runtime stack into that image: `lit`, `LibreOffice`, `ImageMagick`, `fd`, `rg`, `jq`.
- Mount repo root read-only and `/data` writable.
- Define command entrypoints for `sync-repo`, `ingest`, `rebuild-parsed`, `rebuild-routes`, `doctor`.
- Add compose wiring under the `operator` profile.

Implementation notes
- Do not move chat-time retrieval into this service.
- Keep `doc-worker` as a write-path / maintenance component only.
- Resource limits should be higher than `core`, but bounded.

Acceptance criteria
- `docker compose` has a `doc-worker` service under `operator` profile.
- Running the container can execute placeholder commands successfully.
- `core` and `tools-api` do not gain mandatory `lit`/LibreOffice/ImageMagick dependencies.
- Service purpose and operator usage are documented in code or adjacent docs.
close_reason: Implemented doc-worker foundation with operator profile, Docker image, compose wiring, and CLI commands.

title: Live recovery: RFC-026 schema drift and series routing incident (2026-04-27)
description:
Root-cause follow-up for the live 2026-04-27 regression where the agent still failed on:
- `какие у вас есть серии светильников?`
- `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?`

Observed facts from live traces/logs/metrics:
- trace `5f62c80644ffbdec8b1840533e011c28`: broad series request still routes through `corp_db.catalog_lookup` and fails with `400: Unknown knowledge_route_id: corp_db.catalog_lookup`.
- trace `3ce9c0e718635591a5342856eec284c7`: RZD recommendation still fails on `corp_db.sphere_curated_categories` because live Postgres lacks RFC-026 schema objects.
- trace `123a2cf103bece45d6e75c93d67e7ad0`: `portfolio_by_sphere` succeeds, proving corp-db is not globally down.
- `docker compose config --services` includes `corp-db-migrator`, but `docker compose ps -a corp-db-migrator` showed no created runtime container.
- direct DB check returned missing `corp.sphere_curated_categories` and missing `corp.categories.parent_category_id`.

This epic tracks live recovery, runtime redeploy validation, and anti-regression guardrails described in:
`docs/operations/incident-2026-04-27-rfc026-routing-recovery-plan.md`
close_reason: Implemented live recovery, verification, and recurrence guardrails with follow-up blockers closed

title: Recover live RFC-026 corp-db schema with corp-db-migrator and verify health
description:
Live recovery task for the broken RZD recommendation path.

Problem statement:
- In live logs and traces, `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?` still routes to `corp_db.sphere_curated_categories` and fails.
- trace: `3ce9c0e718635591a5342856eec284c7`
- corp-db log error: `отношение "corp.sphere_curated_categories" не существует`
- direct DB check confirmed both RFC-026 objects are missing:
  - `corp.sphere_curated_categories`
  - `corp.categories.parent_category_id`
- `python3 scripts/doctor.py` reports failed RFC-026 schema verification.
- `docker compose ps -a corp-db-migrator` showed that the migrator container had not been created in the live runtime.

Required work:
1. Run the live upgrade path with `corp-db-migrator` against the current persistent volume.
2. Inspect migrator logs and stop if migration fails.
3. Re-check the live DB schema directly with psql.
4. Re-run `python3 scripts/doctor.py` and confirm RFC-026 schema checks pass.
5. Capture operator notes in the issue comment/history if the migration required any manual intervention.

Suggested commands:
- `docker compose up -d --build corp-db corp-db-migrator`
- `docker compose ps -a corp-db-migrator`
- `docker logs corp-db-migrator --tail 200`
- `docker exec corp-db psql -U ${CORP_DB_ADMIN_USER:-postgres} -d ${CORP_DB_NAME:-corp_pg_db} -Atqc "..."`
- `python3 scripts/doctor.py`

Acceptance criteria:
- `corp-db-migrator` exists in compose runtime and completes successfully.
- `to_regclass('corp.sphere_curated_categories')` returns the table name.
- `information_schema.columns` confirms `corp.categories.parent_category_id` exists.
- `python3 scripts/doctor.py` no longer fails on RFC-026 schema drift.
- corp-db logs stop emitting `sphere_curated_categories does not exist` for new requests.
close_reason: Recovered live RFC-026 schema; doctor passes and corp-db-migrator completed successfully

title: Redeploy core and tools-api so live series routing stops using the old catalog_lookup failure path
description:
Live runtime still shows the old broad-series failure path even after repository fixes were committed.

Problem statement:
- trace `5f62c80644ffbdec8b1840533e011c28` for `какие у вас есть серии светильников?`
- `core` selected `selected_route_id=corp_db.catalog_lookup`
- `tools-api` returned `400: {"detail":"Unknown knowledge_route_id: corp_db.catalog_lookup"}`
- fallback then produced an empty/ungrounded result and the user got the degraded reply:
  `Сейчас у меня нет подтверждённого списка серий в корпоративной базе: по этому запросу данные не нашлись.`

Interpretation:
- The running `core` / `tools-api` containers are still behaving like the pre-fix runtime.
- Live services must be rebuilt/restarted only after RFC-026 schema recovery so the runtime actually uses the committed routing fixes.

Required work:
1. Rebuild and restart `tools-api` and `core` after the DB schema task is complete.
2. If compose does not recreate the containers, force remove and recreate them.
3. Verify that new broad-series requests no longer go through the broken `corp_db.catalog_lookup` + invalid `knowledge_route_id` path.
4. Capture the new trace ID for a post-redeploy series request.

Suggested commands:
- `docker compose up -d --build tools-api core`
- if needed: `docker compose rm -sf core tools-api && docker compose up -d --build tools-api core`
- `docker compose ps core tools-api`
- `docker logs core --tail 100`
- `docker logs tools-api --tail 100`

Acceptance criteria:
- live `core` and `tools-api` are recreated from the current working tree / image build.
- a fresh broad-series request does not produce `Unknown knowledge_route_id: corp_db.catalog_lookup`.
- a fresh broad-series request does not end in the old degraded `нет подтверждённого списка серий` fallback unless a new independently diagnosed issue appears.
- evidence (trace ID and relevant log lines) is recorded in the issue.
close_reason: Redeployed core/tools-api and verified fresh series trace b717d4403d13f3c9b39bb0f13ed8ba18 avoids old catalog_lookup failure path

title: Run post-recovery incident verification with traces, logs, metrics, and user prompts
description:
This task validates the live stack after DB migration and application redeploy.

Validation scope:
1. broad series question: `какие у вас есть серии светильников?`
2. RZD recommendation question: `что порекомендуешь для РЖД?`
3. control question: `Какие есть реализованные проекты для РЖД?`

Why this exists:
- the earlier incident had mixed behavior where `portfolio_by_sphere` worked while `sphere_curated_categories` and broad series still failed.
- recovery is only complete if traces, logs, metrics, and user-visible responses all converge to the expected healthy state.

Required work:
1. Run the three live prompts against the stack.
2. Capture the new trace IDs.
3. Verify VictoriaTraces spans for the repaired routes.
4. Verify VictoriaLogs / container logs no longer contain:
   - `Unknown knowledge_route_id: corp_db.catalog_lookup`
   - `отношение "corp.sphere_curated_categories" не существует`
   - `tool_status=error` for `selected_route_id=corp_db.sphere_curated_categories`
5. Verify post-fix metrics over a fresh window:
   - `http_server_duration_milliseconds_count{service_name="tools-api",route="/corp-db/search"}`
   - `corp_db_search_duration_milliseconds_count`
   - `tool_executions_total{tool_name="corp_db_search"}`
6. Update the issue with the final validation summary and exact trace IDs.

Acceptance criteria:
- series query no longer triggers the old `corp_db.catalog_lookup` -> `Unknown knowledge_route_id` failure.
- RZD recommendation no longer degrades to `корпоративная база временно недоступна` because of missing RFC-026 schema.
- RZD portfolio query remains successful.
- fresh metrics show no new `sphere_curated_categories:error` events in the validation window.
- fresh logs show no new `corp.sphere_curated_categories does not exist` errors.
close_reason: Validated live traces b717d4403d13f3c9b39bb0f13ed8ba18, a36b1061569b8efe7d9260e1fb672a25, and b21a3d206c5e63a093f964dad151a67e; no fresh RFC-026 schema errors observed

title: Add deploy guardrails and runtime visibility to prevent RFC-026 runtime drift recurrence
description:
Prevent recurrence of the 2026-04-27 live/runtime drift incident.

Observed recurrence drivers:
- repository fixes were committed, but live runtime still behaved as if the old path was deployed;
- `corp-db-migrator` existed in compose config but had not been created/run in the live stack;
- the incident was detected only after user-facing queries failed again.

Required work:
1. Define and implement an explicit deploy gate for RFC-026 live schema state.
2. Ensure deploy/runbook steps make `corp-db-migrator` a first-class required upgrade stage.
3. Require post-deploy validation using:
   - `python3 scripts/doctor.py`
   - `python3 scripts/incident_replay_smoke.py`
   - targeted chat smoke for broad series and RZD recommendation
4. Add runtime version/build visibility so operators can quickly confirm what is actually deployed:
   - git SHA
   - build time
   - routing catalog version
   - schema/RFC-026 applied indicator
5. Update operational docs with the mandatory sequence and rollback/stop conditions.

Acceptance criteria:
- there is a documented and enforced recovery/deploy sequence that prevents `core/tools-api` from silently running ahead of an unmigrated corp-db volume.
- operators can tell from runtime-visible signals whether current containers include the expected routing fixes.
- post-deploy validation steps are documented and practical enough to run immediately after recovery.
- docs link back to `docs/operations/incident-2026-04-27-rfc026-routing-recovery-plan.md` where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented runtime build/schema visibility, deploy runbook guardrails, and practical incident replay smoke validation

title: Fix RFC-026 live migration to tolerate orphan category parent refs in categories.json
description:
Discovered while executing `totosha-ua30.1` live recovery.

Observed blocker:
- `corp-db-migrator` now creates and starts, but exits with code 1 while running `ensure-rfc026`.
- exact error from `docker logs corp-db-migrator`:
  `asyncpg.exceptions.ForeignKeyViolationError: INSERT или UPDATE в таблице "categories" нарушает ограничение внешнего ключа "categories_parent_category_id_fkey"`
  `DETAIL: Ключ (parent_category_id)=(159) отсутствует в таблице "categories".`
- source inspection shows `db/categories.json` contains one orphan parent reference: category `168` points to parent `159`, but category `159` is absent from the canonical category ID set.

Why this matters:
- RFC-026 live migration cannot complete on the current canonical source set.
- This blocks both the live schema recovery task and any deploy flow that requires `corp-db-migrator` to complete successfully before `tools-api` starts.

Required work:
1. Make RFC-026 live migration resilient to orphan parent references in `db/categories.json`.
2. Choose and implement the safest behavior:
   - either skip unresolved parent assignments and leave `parent_category_id = NULL`, or
   - validate and sanitize parent assignments before updating `corp.categories`.
3. Add tests that cover a source payload containing at least one missing parent ID.
4. Rebuild/re-run `corp-db-migrator` and confirm the live migration completes successfully on the current source set.

Acceptance criteria:
- migration no longer crashes when a category points to a missing parent ID in source JSON.
- resulting live DB keeps valid parent links and does not create FK-invalid rows.
- tests cover orphan parent references.
- `corp-db-migrator` completes successfully on the live stack after the fix.
close_reason: Implemented: sanitized orphan parent refs, added tests, and reran corp-db-migrator successfully

title: Fix rebuilt tools-api startup hang caused by Python bytecode-cache writes
description:
Discovered while executing `totosha-ua30.2` live redeploy.

Observed blocker:
- freshly rebuilt `totosha-tools-api` container does not become healthy after redeploy.
- `docker inspect tools-api` health logs show repeated `curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8100`.
- debugging with `docker run -i --rm totosha-tools-api python - <<'PY' ... import app ... PY` shows import hanging during Python bytecode cache writes:
  stack ends in `importlib._bootstrap_external._write_atomic` while importing `prometheus_client` through `src/observability.py`.

Why this matters:
- the service process exists, but startup never reaches a listening state;
- this blocks `tools-api` health, which blocks `core` startup and prevents incident recovery verification.

Required work:
1. Remove or bypass bytecode-cache writes during container startup/import for Python services affected by this behavior.
2. Verify `tools-api` image can import `app` promptly in a one-shot container.
3. Rebuild and redeploy `tools-api` (and any other affected Python service image if needed) until healthchecks pass.
4. Record the root cause and validation evidence in the issue state.

Acceptance criteria:
- `docker run -i --rm <image> python -c 'import app'` style import no longer hangs.
- `tools-api` reaches healthy state after redeploy.
- dependent `core` startup can proceed normally.
close_reason: Implemented: disabled bytecode-cache writes in Python service images and restored tools-api health

title: Reduce route-selector prompt size so first-turn requests fit context budget
description:
Discovered while verifying `totosha-ua30.2` after redeploy.

Observed blocker:
- fresh `/api/chat` requests for `Какие у вас есть серии светильников?` no longer hit the old `corp_db.catalog_lookup` path, but they still fail before route selection.
- exact live core log for trace `aa111a088bd452b14909684ca925cbc9`:
  - `Context trim hard stop stage=route_selector pre=43276 post=43276 budget=40000 removed=0 truncated=0 reason=protected_context_exceeds_budget`
  - `Route selector unavailable or invalid: Context exceeds MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS (43276 > 40000) and cannot be trimmed safely`
- the user receives the generic degraded reply `Извините, сервис сейчас временно недоступен...`.

Why this matters:
- this blocks validation of the repaired series routing path because the route selector prompt itself is too large to send.
- it also means first-turn requests can fail even with an empty chat history.

Required work:
1. Reduce route-selector prompt size so first-turn payloads fit safely under `MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS`.
2. Prefer a compact projection for selector prompts rather than sending the full route catalog JSON verbatim.
3. Add regression coverage that builds selector messages for a representative series query and confirms the prompt stays within budget / is materially smaller.
4. Redeploy `core` and verify fresh series requests reach route selection instead of failing at `route_selector_unavailable`.

Acceptance criteria:
- first-turn selector prompt for a representative broad-series query no longer exceeds the context budget.
- fresh series requests do not return `route_selector_unavailable` due to prompt size.
- validation evidence includes a fresh trace ID after redeploy.
close_reason: Implemented: compacted selector payload and removed route-selector budget failure on first-turn requests

title: Restrict broad-series selector shortlist to safe non-entity routes
description:
Discovered during `totosha-ua30.2` verification after fixing the route-selector budget.

Observed blocker:
- fresh broad-series request no longer fails at `route_selector_unavailable`, but the selector still picks a structured corp-db route instead of the authoritative broad-series KB path.
- live traces/logs after the redeploy show:
  - first it picked `corp_db.catalog_lookup` and failed with `Missing field: name`
  - after narrowing candidates, it picked `corp_db.category_lamps` and failed with `Missing field: category`
- this means the selector is still allowed to choose routes that require entity-specific arguments for a broad, list-all-series question.

Why this matters:
- the original incident was about broad series questions being routed into structured catalog/entity routes that require a specific `name` / `category` / similar argument.
- broad series questions should use the authoritative company-common KB path or another route that does not require an entity-specific key.

Required work:
1. Make broad-series selector candidate generation restrictive enough that first-turn broad series questions do not offer entity-specific structured routes as primary choices.
2. Prefer a single authoritative broad-series route when appropriate (`corp_kb.company_common`) so the LLM cannot bounce into `catalog_lookup` / `category_lamps` for generic listing asks.
3. Update tests to lock this behavior.
4. Redeploy `core` and verify a fresh broad-series request does not hit `corp_db.catalog_lookup` or `corp_db.category_lamps` in logs/traces.

Acceptance criteria:
- fresh broad-series requests do not produce `Missing field: name` or `Missing field: category`.
- fresh broad-series requests do not select `corp_db.catalog_lookup` or `corp_db.category_lamps` as the primary selector route.
- tests cover the restricted shortlist behavior.
close_reason: Implemented: broad-series selector shortlist now restricts to company_common and avoids structured entity-route failures

title: Route broad-object catalog questions to application_recommendation in prompt and skills
description:
Source: RFC-008

Update orchestration rules so supported object-based queries consistently use the new fast path instead of generic hybrid_search. This task owns prompt and skill behavior, not backend ranking.

Scope:
- update core/src/agent/system.txt with a special-case rule for broad-object recommendation questions
- update shared_skills/skills/corp-pg-db/SKILL.md to document the new kind and the expected answer contract
- require application_recommendation before generic hybrid_search for wording such as stadium, quarry, airport, warehouse, office, and aggressive-environment requests
- define terminal behavior after successful application_recommendation so the agent does not continue searching
- document that the first answer must include links/materials and one follow-up question

Acceptance:
- the agent routes supported broad-object prompts to application_recommendation
- after success the agent does not call hybrid_search, doc_search, or shell-style fallbacks for the same answer
- prompt and skill instructions are consistent with the backend contract
close_reason: Updated prompt, skill guidance, and core guardrails to route broad-object questions to application_recommendation

title: Add telemetry for runtime finalizer mode and payload format

title: RFC-027 remediation epic: close architecture, schema, observability, and runtime gaps
description:
## Context
Ревью показало, что реализация и сопутствующая observability для RFC-027 закрывают только часть целевого контракта. В коде остаются четыре класса замечаний:
1. До LLM всё ещё применяется детерминированный shortlist leaf-маршрутов, что противоречит family-first модели RFC-027.
2. Route-local schemas по-прежнему смешивают пользовательские аргументы с internal executor/locked args (`kind`, `fuzzy`, `profile`, `knowledge_route_id` и др.).
3. Логи и observability задекларированы шире, чем реально эмитятся в текстовый log formatter и smoke checks.
4. Runtime flow регрессировал: при недоступности/выключении route selector'а агент массово возвращает generic unavailable response, что ломает guardrail/runtime тесты.

## Goal
Довести реализацию до полного и проверяемого соответствия RFC-027 без потери текущих guardrail-свойств и без регресса существующего runtime поведения.

## Workstreams
- family-first selector contract и отказ от brittle pre-LLM shortlist;
- route-local schema narrowing и отделение locked args от selector-visible args;
- observability/logging/smoke alignment по family/leaf/stage/validation/fallback полям;
- runtime stability и controlled degradation без массового fail-closed регресса;
- acceptance/replay/integration coverage для критериев RFC-027.

## Done when
Все дочерние задачи закрыты, acceptance criteria RFC-027 покрыты тестами и smoke, а guardrail/runtime тесты снова зелёные.
close_reason: All RFC-027 remediation workstreams completed: family-first selector payload, selector-visible schema split, LLM-only runtime semantics, observability alignment, and focused regression/verification coverage.

title: RFC-027: replace brittle pre-LLM route shortlist with family-first selector payload
description:
## Problem
Текущая реализация selector payload по-прежнему формируется через deterministic intent shortlist (`_ordered_routes_for_intent(... )[:max_routes]`), а для broad series есть даже принудительное урезание кандидатов до одного route. Это нарушает RFC-027, который требует family-first routing без brittle exact-leaf shortlist до LLM.

## Scope
1. Перестроить selector payload так, чтобы production path презентовал LLM стабильную карту семейств и leaf routes внутри семейств, а не заранее урезанный Python shortlist exact routes.
2. Сохранить compact payload и budget control, но делать ограничение через family-level presentation/compaction, а не через скрытие корректных leaf routes до LLM.
3. Убрать/переработать special-case logic, которая принудительно схлопывает broad series до одного route.
4. Явно документировать допустимые deterministic pre-processing steps: alias resolution, canonical context injection, sphere context, но не hiding exact routes.
5. Проверить, что fallback остаётся family-local по умолчанию и что selector validation по-прежнему запрещает неявные cross-family fallbacks.

## Acceptance criteria
- Selector payload для production path больше не зависит от brittle pre-LLM exact-leaf shortlist.
- LLM получает families + leaf routes grouped by family как основной контракт выбора.
- Broad series, documents, codes, sphere-category и portfolio не ограничиваются скрытым exact-route shortlist'ом до выбора семейства.
- RFC-027 acceptance criteria #1 и #2 закрыты тестами.
- В routing tests появляются явные проверки, что payload/selection остаются family-first даже при compact mode.

## Notes
Эта задача меняет routing contract и должна быть выполнена до финального acceptance-smoke по RFC-027.
close_reason: Implemented family-first selector payload with grouped families/leaf routes and compact-mode coverage in routing tests.

title: RFC-027: narrow route-local schemas and separate locked args from selector-visible args
description:
## Problem
Route-local schemas всё ещё содержат internal executor and locked fields (`kind`, `fuzzy`, `profile`, `knowledge_route_id`, `source_files` и др.) и местами делают их required. Это противоречит RFC-027: selector должен генерировать только user-meaningful args, а execution scope должен задаваться locked/template args отдельно.

## Scope
1. Пересмотреть `default_argument_schema`, `_infer_locked_args`, route allowlists и runtime overrides так, чтобы selector-visible schema содержала только user-facing поля.
2. Убрать требование `kind` из user-facing schemas там, где это executor detail, а не business argument.
3. Перепроверить схемы для обязательных leaf routes RFC-027:
   - `series_description`
   - `documents_by_lamp_name`
   - `sku_codes_lookup`
   - `showcase_lamps_by_category`
   - `portfolio_projects_by_sphere`
   - `mountings_by_category`
   - `curated_categories_by_sphere`
4. Сохранить `additionalProperties: false`, compact enums и validator safety.
5. Развести contract-level schema и execution-level merged args так, чтобы locked args продолжали применяться в рантайме, но не требовались от selector output.

## Acceptance criteria
- Selector-visible schemas содержат только route-meaningful user arguments.
- Internal executor/locked args больше не являются required user fields в route-local schemas.
- Все active leaf routes сохраняют `additionalProperties: false`.
- RFC-027 acceptance criterion #3 закрыт тестами на schema shape и arg merging.
- Regression tests покрывают merge order: defaults -> selector tool_args -> locked_args.

## Dependencies
Задача может вестись параллельно с переработкой family-first selector payload, но должна быть завершена до финального acceptance smoke и перед донастройкой observability validation labels.
close_reason: Implemented selector-visible vs execution schema split, locked-arg merge contract, and schema/merge regression coverage.

title: RFC-027 correction: remove deterministic final-answer renderers from successful runtime flows
description:
## Context
The clarified product rule is stronger than outage handling alone: the LLM must produce **every** user-visible final answer, not just answers in outage-free cases. Review of `core/agent.py` shows several deterministic rendering paths (`deterministic_primary`, `deterministic_fallback`, company-fact fast paths, `_render_deterministic_tool_output`, `_render_generic_kb_payload`) that can bypass the finalizer LLM even when the routed request succeeds.

## Problem
Successful routed flows still have architecture-divergent paths where the response is rendered without the finalizer LLM. That preserves two answer semantics:
- LLM-finalized answers;
- deterministic renderer answers.

RFC-027 should keep one answer contract.

## Scope
1. Audit all runtime branches that set or rely on:
   - `finalizer_mode=deterministic_primary`;
   - `finalizer_mode=deterministic_fallback`;
   - `company_fact_finalizer_mode=deterministic_primary|deterministic_fallback`.
2. Route successful company-fact, authoritative KB, documents, portfolio, application, and related flows through `_finalize_with_scoped_evidence(...)` for the final user-visible answer.
3. Remove or disable `_render_deterministic_tool_output(...)` / `_render_generic_kb_payload(...)` as normal runtime answer producers.
4. If deterministic renderers remain for debugging/bench/internal tooling, keep them unreachable from production user answer paths.
5. Simplify/align `finalizer_mode` observability so successful routed requests report `llm` for user-visible answers.

## Acceptance criteria
- every successful routed user-visible answer passes through the LLM finalizer;
- no production runtime path returns a normal answer via deterministic renderers;
- representative tests assert `finalizer_mode == "llm"` for successful company fact, KB, document, portfolio, and application flows;
- any remaining deterministic renderers are internal-only and documented as such.
close_reason: Removed deterministic final-answer runtime paths, kept successful routed answers on LLM finalization, and added focused regression coverage for runtime/benchmark/application/document/portfolio flows.

title: doc_search resilience: isolate malformed document failures per file
description:
Context
The new `doc_search` engine performs local extraction across live and legacy documents. Today extraction helpers for ZIP-based Office files and parser-on-demand flows can raise exceptions, and the main search loop does not isolate those failures per document.

Problem
- A single malformed `docx`, `xlsx`, `pptx`, broken archive, parser timeout edge case, or unexpected extraction exception can abort the entire `doc_search` request.
- In a shared corporate corpus this makes retrieval brittle: one bad file can break unrelated queries.

Scope
- Add per-document exception isolation around extraction/parsing.
- Distinguish parser failure from ordinary no-match in the returned telemetry and logs.
- Preserve successful results from other documents in the same request.
- Add regression tests with malformed fixtures proving that search continues.

Implementation notes
- Prefer a narrow `try/except` around extraction of each document rather than swallowing unrelated control-flow errors globally.
- Emit enough telemetry to debug bad documents without crashing the whole search.
- Keep result contract backward-compatible where possible.

Acceptance criteria
- One malformed document does not fail the overall `doc_search` request.
- Search returns results from healthy documents in the same corpus.
- Parser/extraction failures are observable and distinguishable from empty search results.
- Regression tests cover malformed Office/XML and parser-failure scenarios.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Operations remediation: restore valid observability compose override
description:
Problem

- The override file is truncated after the `corp-db-worker:` key, so operator restarts cannot re-apply the observability profile described in RFC-011 / runbooks.
- We need a valid override that restores OTEL env and localhost port bindings for the app services without changing unrelated base-compose behavior.

Scope
- Repair `docker-compose.observability.yml` so every listed service override is a valid mapping.
- Restore the `corp-db-worker` override block with the same observability environment contract used by the other services and expected by the docs/runbooks.
- Verify the file with `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.observability.yml config` and a real `up -d` against the observability stack.
- Keep the change limited to the compose override and any directly related documentation only if required by the final shape.

Acceptance Criteria
- `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.observability.yml config` exits successfully.
- `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.observability.yml up -d` no longer fails on schema validation.
- `corp-db-worker` receives the same OTEL env family as the other services in the override file.

title: RFC-007: multi-format tests and bench regression gates
description:
Context
RFC-007 changes retrieval behavior, storage, parsing and routing. We need regression gates across correctness, latency and routing so the new document path does not silently regress like the old wiki path did.

Scope
- Add unit tests for file type detection, CAS path derivation, dedup by content hash, backend selection and cache invalidation.
- Add integration tests for multi-format retrieval (`md`, `pdf`, `docx`, `xlsx`, `pptx`, `png`, `jpg`, `tiff`).
- Add tests proving identical uploads reuse the same CAS blob and parse cache.
- Extend bench dataset/eval with doc_search routing and latency assertions.
- Cover guardrail behavior: short company facts stay on corp-db unless explicit document context is requested.

Implementation notes
- Prefer small representative fixtures over large corpora.
- Separate answer-quality failures from routing/security/parser failures in bench output.
- Historical runs should remain analyzable even if canonical naming changes.

Acceptance criteria
- CI catches regressions in dedup, retrieval routing and parser behavior.
- Bench covers new multi-format document scenarios.
- Routing assertions distinguish corp-db hot path from doc_search fallback path.
- Duplicate-upload and cache-sharing behavior is verified automatically.
close_reason: Added multi-format storage/search/security tests, bench routing alias handling, and updated golden routing assertions for doc_search.

Acceptance:

- application cases validate intent separation and catch regressions such as low-power indoor results for stadium-style prompts

title: proxy: startup without secrets leaves /v1/* in 'LLM not configured' state until restart
description: Observed on 2026-03-27 during runtime verification. External egress works (api.openai.com reachable, status.openai.com 200), but local proxy returns 500 {"error":"LLM not configured"} for /v1/models, /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings. Current proxy container has secret files mounted now, but startup logs show base_url/api_key/model_name were not found at process init, so LLM_BASE_URL stayed unset for the lifetime of the process. Need fail-fast or reload behavior when secrets are missing at startup, and startup/health signals that distinguish healthy HTTP listener from usable LLM proxy.
close_reason: Fixed proxy secret loading and readiness: proxy now runs with readable secret UID, reloads secrets at request time, exposes /ready, and reports usable LLM state separately from liveness.
